title: Le français en route vers le DELF

days.title: Le subjonctif : nuances (bien que, quoique, pour que)
days.objective: Master the subjunctive after concessive and purpose conjunctions to express nuance in argumentation.
days.paretoFocus: Lock in bien que / quoique / pour que + subjunctive, the highest-frequency B2 triggers.
drills: Write 5 sentences using bien que + subjunctive about your daily routine.
Transform 5 indicative clauses into concessive clauses with quoique.
Say aloud 3 goals using pour que + subjunctive with different subjects.

items: Recall the three conjunctions that always require the subjunctive today.
Conjugate faire, pouvoir and savoir in the present subjunctive.
Write one sentence contrasting bien que and parce que.

activities.title: Concession et but au subjonctif
activities.intro: These conjunctions systematically trigger the subjunctive; note the different subjects between clauses.

items.hr: Bien qu'il pleuve, nous sortirons.
items.en: Although it is raining, we will go out.

items.hr: Quoiqu'elle soit fatiguée, elle continue.
items.en: Even though she is tired, she carries on.

items.hr: Je t'explique pour que tu comprennes.
items.en: I am explaining so that you understand.

items.hr: Bien que nous ayons peu de temps, finissons.
items.en: Although we have little time, let's finish.

items.hr: Quoi qu'il fasse, je le soutiens.
items.en: Whatever he does, I support him.

activities.title: Choisir le mode
activities.intro: Decide which mood each conjunction below demands, then supply the form.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence.
options: Bien qu'il est malade, il travaille.
Bien qu'il soit malade, il travaille.
Bien qu'il sera malade, il travaille.
Bien qu'il a été malade, il travaille.
questions.answer: Bien qu'il soit malade, il travaille.
questions.explanation: Bien que requires the subjunctive: être becomes soit.

questions.prompt: Which conjunction expresses purpose and takes the subjunctive?
questions.explanation: Pour que introduces a goal and always triggers the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Fill in with venir (subjunctive): 'Je t'appelle pour que tu ___.'
questions.explanation: Pour que + subjunctive; venir gives tu viennes.

questions.prompt: Fill in with savoir (subjunctive): 'Quoiqu'il ne ___ rien, il donne son avis.'
questions.explanation: Quoique requires the subjunctive; savoir gives il sache.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Although I'm busy, I will come.'
questions.explanation: The concessive clause with the subjunctive precedes the main future clause.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Explain it to him so that he understands better.'
questions.explanation: Pour que introduces the subjunctive comprenne expressing purpose.

activities.title: Un désaccord poli
activities.intro: You play 'Me'; concede a point while defending your view using the subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu penses vraiment qu'on devrait accepter ce projet ?
lines.en: Do you really think we should accept this project?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien que ce soit risqué, je crois que ça vaut la peine.
lines.en: Although it is risky, I believe it is worth it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quoique tu dises, les coûts m'inquiètent.
lines.en: Whatever you say, the costs worry me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je t'en parle justement pour que nous trouvions une solution.
lines.en: I am talking to you precisely so that we find a solution.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord, bien que je reste prudent.
lines.en: All right, although I remain cautious.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, quoiqu'il faille encore convaincre l'équipe.
lines.en: Perfect, even though we still have to convince the team.

days.title: Le subjonctif passé
days.objective: Use the past subjunctive to express a completed action within a subjunctive context.
days.paretoFocus: Form avoir/être in present subjunctive plus past participle to signal anteriority.
drills: Write 5 sentences with 'Je suis content que...' + past subjunctive.
Convert 5 present-subjunctive sentences into the past subjunctive.
Say 3 sentences using 'bien que' + past subjunctive about last week.

items: Recall how the past subjunctive is formed.
Give the past subjunctive of partir and finir (il/elle).
Write one sentence with 'Je doute que tu aies...'.

activities.title: Antériorité au subjonctif
activities.intro: The past subjunctive marks an action completed before the main verb; auxiliary agreement rules still apply.

items.hr: Je suis content que tu sois venu.
items.en: I am glad you came.

items.hr: Il est possible qu'elle ait déjà répondu.
items.en: It is possible she has already answered.

items.hr: Bien qu'ils aient fini, ils vérifient encore.
items.en: Although they have finished, they are still checking.

items.hr: Je doute qu'il soit parti si tôt.
items.en: I doubt he left so early.

items.hr: C'est dommage que vous ne soyez pas restés.
items.en: It is a pity you did not stay.

activities.title: Former le passé du subjonctif
activities.intro: Build the past subjunctive with the correct auxiliary, agreement and word order.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct past subjunctive sentence.
options: Je suis surpris qu'il a réussi.
Je suis surpris qu'il ait réussi.
Je suis surpris qu'il aurait réussi.
Je suis surpris qu'il avait réussi.
questions.answer: Je suis surpris qu'il ait réussi.
questions.explanation: After an emotion verb, use the past subjunctive: ait réussi.

questions.prompt: Which auxiliary form is the subjunctive of être (3rd person singular)?
questions.explanation: The present subjunctive of être (il/elle) is soit, used to form the past subjunctive of être-verbs.

questions.prompt: Fill in the auxiliary (subjunctive of être): 'Je suis heureux qu'elle ___ arrivée.'
questions.explanation: Arriver takes être; the subjunctive auxiliary is soit, with agreement arrivée.

questions.prompt: Fill in with avoir (subjunctive): 'Bien que nous ___ terminé, on relit.'
questions.explanation: Terminer takes avoir; subjunctive nous form is ayons.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I'm sorry you left so quickly.'
questions.explanation: Regretter triggers the past subjunctive sois parti expressing a completed action.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'She may have forgotten the appointment.'
questions.explanation: Il se peut que requires the subjunctive; ait oublié marks anteriority.

activities.title: Réagir à des nouvelles
activities.intro: You play 'Me'; react to past events using the past subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: L'équipe a rendu le rapport hier soir.
lines.en: The team handed in the report last night.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis ravi qu'ils l'aient terminé à temps.
lines.en: I am delighted they finished it on time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mais Paul n'était pas présent.
lines.en: But Paul was not there.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est dommage qu'il ne soit pas venu.
lines.en: It is a pity he did not come.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Penses-tu qu'ils aient tout vérifié ?
lines.en: Do you think they checked everything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je doute qu'ils aient eu le temps de tout relire.
lines.en: I doubt they had time to reread it all.

days.title: La concordance des temps
days.objective: Align verb tenses correctly across main and subordinate clauses in past narration and reported speech.
days.paretoFocus: Map present-context and past-context sequences so tenses shift consistently.
drills: Convert 5 direct-speech sentences into reported speech in the past.
Rewrite 5 present-tense main clauses into a past-tense main clause, adjusting the subordinate.
Narrate yesterday in 4 sentences using imparfait and plus-que-parfait together.

items: Recall which tense replaces the present in reported past speech.
State how the future shifts after a past reporting verb.
Write one sentence pairing plus-que-parfait with imparfait.

activities.title: L'enchaînement des temps
activities.intro: The main verb's tense governs the subordinate; learn the standard shifts in past contexts and reported speech.

items.hr: Il dit qu'il viendra demain.
items.en: He says he will come tomorrow.

items.hr: Il a dit qu'il viendrait le lendemain.
items.en: He said he would come the next day.

items.hr: Je pensais qu'elle était déjà partie.
items.en: I thought she had already left.

items.hr: Elle a expliqué qu'elle travaillait à Paris.
items.en: She explained she was working in Paris.

items.hr: Nous savions qu'il avait menti.
items.en: We knew he had lied.
items.note: past knowledge of an earlier action

activities.title: Ajuster les temps
activities.intro: Apply the sequence-of-tenses rules in reported speech and past narration.

questions.prompt: Report in the past: 'Il a dit : Je partirai.' becomes...
options: Il a dit qu'il est parti.
questions.explanation: A future after a past reporting verb becomes the present conditional (future-in-the-past).

questions.prompt: Which tense expresses an action anterior to a past main verb?
questions.explanation: The plus-que-parfait marks an action completed before another past action.

questions.prompt: Fill in with être in imparfait for reported present: 'Elle a dit qu'elle ___ malade.'
questions.explanation: A present in direct speech shifts to the imparfait after a past reporting verb.

questions.prompt: Fill in the plus-que-parfait of finir: 'Je croyais que tu ___ déjà fini.' (2nd sing., avoir)
questions.explanation: Plus-que-parfait = imparfait of avoir (avais) + past participle fini.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'He promised he would help us the next day.'
questions.explanation: The future aider becomes the conditional aiderait after the past promettre.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'We didn't know she had moved.'
questions.explanation: The plus-que-parfait avait déménagé shows anteriority to the past ignorions.

activities.title: Raconter une réunion
activities.intro: You play 'Me'; report what was said, adjusting tenses to the past.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que le directeur a annoncé ?
lines.en: What did the director announce?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il a annoncé qu'il lancerait un nouveau projet.
lines.en: He announced he would launch a new project.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et il a parlé du budget ?
lines.en: And did he talk about the budget?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il a précisé que le budget avait déjà été validé.
lines.en: He specified that the budget had already been approved.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Les collègues étaient d'accord ?
lines.en: Were the colleagues in agreement?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ils pensaient que la décision arrivait trop vite.
lines.en: They thought the decision was coming too fast.

days.title: Le conditionnel passé & l'hypothèse irréelle du passé
days.objective: Express regret and unreal past hypotheses using si + plus-que-parfait and the past conditional.
days.paretoFocus: Cement the pattern Si + plus-que-parfait, conditionnel passé for irreality in the past.
drills: Write 5 regrets using 'J'aurais dû + infinitive'.
Complete 5 unreal-past sentences: 'Si j'avais su, ...'.
Say 3 counterfactuals about your own past choices aloud.

items: Recall the full si-clause structure for the unreal past.
Form the past conditional of pouvoir and devoir (je).
Write one sentence expressing a regret with the past conditional.

activities.title: L'irréel du passé
activities.intro: The unreal past imagines an outcome that did not happen; pair si + plus-que-parfait with the past conditional.

items.hr: Si j'avais su, je serais venu.
items.en: If I had known, I would have come.

items.hr: Tu aurais réussi si tu avais révisé.
items.en: You would have passed if you had studied.

items.hr: J'aurais dû partir plus tôt.
items.en: I should have left earlier.

items.hr: Nous aurions pu l'éviter.
items.en: We could have avoided it.

items.hr: S'ils étaient partis à l'heure, ils n'auraient pas raté le train.
items.en: If they had left on time, they would not have missed the train.
items.note: negative outcome in the past conditional

activities.title: Exprimer l'irréel
activities.intro: Rewrite what actually happened as what might have been, choosing the right tense for each clause.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct unreal-past sentence.
options: Si j'aurais su, je serais venu.
Si j'avais su, je serais venu.
Si je savais, je serais venu.
questions.answer: Si j'avais su, je serais venu.
questions.explanation: The pattern is si + plus-que-parfait followed by the past conditional; never conditional after si.

questions.prompt: Which structure expresses a regret about the past?
questions.explanation: The past conditional of devoir (aurais dû) expresses regret or reproach.

questions.prompt: Fill in the plus-que-parfait of prévenir (2nd sing.): 'Si tu m'___ prévenu, j'aurais agi.'
questions.explanation: Plus-que-parfait = avais + prévenu in the si-clause of an unreal past.

questions.prompt: Fill in the past conditional of pouvoir (nous): 'Nous ___ pu venir plus tôt.'
questions.explanation: Past conditional = conditional of avoir (aurions) + pu.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'If you had run, you would have caught the bus.'
questions.explanation: Si + plus-que-parfait (avais couru) then the past conditional (aurais attrapé).

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I would have preferred you to tell me.'
questions.explanation: The past conditional aurais préféré governs the subjunctive dises.

activities.title: Des regrets après coup
activities.intro: You play 'Me'; express regrets and counterfactuals about a missed event.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu n'es pas venu à la conférence hier.
lines.en: You did not come to the conference yesterday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si j'avais su qu'elle était importante, j'y serais allé.
lines.en: If I had known it was important, I would have gone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le conférencier a parlé de ton domaine.
lines.en: The speaker talked about your field.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'aurais dû vérifier le programme plus tôt.
lines.en: I should have checked the program earlier.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On aurait pu y aller ensemble.
lines.en: We could have gone together.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si tu m'avais prévenu, nous aurions organisé le trajet.
lines.en: If you had told me, we would have arranged the trip.

days.title: Le passif & la forme pronominale à sens passif
days.objective: Form the passive voice and use pronominal constructions with passive meaning naturally.
days.paretoFocus: Choose between être + past participle and the se-passive to sound idiomatic.
drills: Transform 5 active sentences into the passive voice with être.
Rewrite 5 passive sentences using a pronominal passive (se + verb).
Describe 3 everyday facts using 'ça se dit / ça se fait / ça se vend'.

items: Recall when to use par vs de with the agent.
Give the passive of 'On construit une école.'
Write one sentence using a se-passive.

activities.title: Deux façons d'exprimer le passif
activities.intro: The passive uses être + past participle with agreement; the pronominal passive avoids naming an agent.

items.hr: La loi a été votée par le Parlement.
items.en: The law was passed by Parliament.

items.hr: Il est respecté de tous.
items.en: He is respected by everyone.

items.hr: Ce produit se vend très bien.
items.en: This product sells very well.

items.hr: Cela ne se dit pas en français soutenu.
items.en: That is not said in formal French.

items.hr: Les résultats seront publiés demain.
items.en: The results will be published tomorrow.

activities.title: Actif, passif, pronominal
activities.intro: Transform structures and choose the right passive form with correct agreement.

questions.prompt: Passive of 'On a signé les contrats.'
questions.explanation: Passive = être + past participle agreeing with the subject (signés, masc. plural).

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses a pronominal passive correctly?
options: Ce plat se prépare avec du safran.
Ce plat est prépare avec du safran.
Ce plat a préparé du safran.
questions.answer: Ce plat se prépare avec du safran.
questions.explanation: The se-passive uses a conjugated pronominal verb without a stated agent.

questions.prompt: Complete the passive with être (present) + agreement: 'La maison ___ vendue.'
questions.explanation: Passive present = est + vendue, agreeing with the feminine subject.

questions.prompt: Rewrite as a se-passive: 'On parle français ici.' -> 'Le français ___ parle ici.' (fill the pronoun)
questions.explanation: The pronominal passive uses se + verb: le français se parle ici.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The report was written by the team.'
questions.explanation: Passé composé passive: a été rédigé, with the agent introduced by par.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'This kind of mistake is easily corrected.'
questions.explanation: The se-passive corrige expresses a general truth without an agent.

activities.title: Présenter une entreprise
activities.intro: You play 'Me'; describe how things are done using passive forms.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment vos produits sont-ils fabriqués ?
lines.en: How are your products made?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ils sont fabriqués en France par des artisans.
lines.en: They are made in France by craftsmen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la vente, comment ça marche ?
lines.en: And sales, how does it work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nos articles se vendent surtout en ligne.
lines.en: Our items sell mainly online.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Les prix seront-ils modifiés bientôt ?
lines.en: Will the prices be changed soon?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ils seront révisés au printemps, mais rien ne se décide sans étude.
lines.en: They will be revised in spring, but nothing is decided without a study.

days.title: Le participe présent (à l'écrit)
days.objective: Use the present participle and the gérondif to condense clauses in formal writing.
days.paretoFocus: Distinguish the participe présent, the gérondif (en + -ant), and adjectival forms.
drills: Rewrite 5 relative clauses using a present participle.
Turn 5 'parce que' clauses into causal present participles.
Write 3 sentences with the gérondif expressing simultaneity.

items: Recall how the present participle is formed from the nous-form.
State the difference between a participe présent and a verbal adjective.
Write one sentence with en + present participle.

activities.title: Participe présent et gérondif
activities.intro: The present participle (invariable) replaces relative or causal clauses; the gérondif adds en for manner or simultaneity.

items.hr: Les étudiants souhaitant participer doivent s'inscrire.
items.en: Students wishing to participate must register.

items.hr: Étant malade, il est resté chez lui.
items.en: Being ill, he stayed home.
items.note: causal value = comme il était malade

items.hr: En travaillant régulièrement, tu progresseras.
items.en: By working regularly, you will progress.

items.hr: Elle lit en écoutant de la musique.
items.en: She reads while listening to music.

items.hr: C'est une histoire touchante.
items.en: It is a touching story.
items.note: verbal adjective agrees, unlike the participle

activities.title: Condenser à l'écrit
activities.intro: Replace clauses with participles and distinguish the gérondif from adjectival forms.

questions.prompt: Rewrite 'les personnes qui possèdent un billet' with a participle.
options: les personnes en possédant un billet
questions.explanation: The present participle possédant is invariable and replaces the relative clause.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the gérondif correctly?
questions.explanation: The gérondif is en + present participle: en voyageant.

questions.prompt: Form the present participle of finir: 'Les candidats ___ tôt peuvent sortir.'
questions.explanation: From the nous-form finissons, drop -ons and add -ant: finissant.

questions.prompt: Complete the gérondif of sourire: 'Elle répond ___.' (en + participle)
questions.explanation: Gérondif = en + present participle souriant, expressing manner.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Having finished her work, she left.'
questions.explanation: The past participial phrase ayant terminé expresses anteriority before the main verb.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'By reading carefully, you will understand better.'
questions.explanation: The gérondif en lisant expresses the condition or means for the result.

activities.title: Rédiger une annonce
activities.intro: You play 'Me'; draft formal notices using present participles.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment formuler l'appel à candidatures ?
lines.en: How should we phrase the call for applications?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Les personnes souhaitant postuler enverront un dossier.
lines.en: People wishing to apply will send a file.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour la clause sur l'expérience ?
lines.en: And for the clause about experience?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Les candidats possédant trois ans d'expérience seront prioritaires.
lines.en: Candidates possessing three years of experience will have priority.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On ajoute un conseil pour l'entretien ?
lines.en: Shall we add advice for the interview?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui : en préparant bien vos réponses, vous augmenterez vos chances.
lines.en: Yes: by preparing your answers well, you will increase your chances.

days.title: Les articulateurs d'argumentation
days.objective: Structure a formal argument using logical connectors of cause, opposition and addition.
days.paretoFocus: Deploy en effet, néanmoins, par ailleurs and en revanche with correct nuance.
drills: Write a 4-sentence paragraph using en effet, par ailleurs, néanmoins and en revanche once each.
Match 5 connectors to their function (cause, addition, opposition, contrast).
Rewrite 3 simple sentences adding an appropriate connector.

items: Recall the difference between néanmoins and en revanche.
State what en effet introduces (confirmation/justification).
Write one sentence using par ailleurs to add an argument.

activities.title: Connecteurs logiques B2
activities.intro: Each connector signals a precise logical move; using them accurately raises the register of your writing.

items.hr: En effet, les chiffres confirment cette tendance.
items.en: Indeed, the figures confirm this trend.
items.note: en effet justifies/confirms, never means 'in fact'-contrast

items.hr: Néanmoins, certaines questions restent en suspens.
items.en: Nevertheless, some questions remain open.

items.hr: Par ailleurs, il faut penser au budget.
items.en: Moreover, we must think about the budget.
items.note: par ailleurs adds a new, distinct point

items.hr: Les ventes montent ; en revanche, les marges baissent.
items.en: Sales are rising; on the other hand, margins are falling.
items.note: en revanche = clear contrast between two facts

items.hr: En effet, sans preuve, l'argument s'effondre.
items.en: Indeed, without proof, the argument collapses.
items.note: introduces the justification of a claim

activities.title: Choisir le bon connecteur
activities.intro: Select connectors that fit the logical relation and the formal register.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces a justification of the previous statement?
questions.explanation: En effet confirms and justifies what precedes; it is not adversative.

questions.prompt: Choose the connector expressing a clear contrast between two facts.
questions.explanation: En revanche opposes two contrasting facts symmetrically.

questions.prompt: Insert the connector meaning 'moreover / besides': 'Le projet est coûteux. ___, il est risqué.'
questions.explanation: Par ailleurs adds a further, distinct argument.

questions.prompt: Insert the concessive connector (synonym of cependant): 'Le plan est solide ; ___, des doutes subsistent.'
questions.explanation: Néanmoins introduces a concession, like cependant/toutefois.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Indeed, the data confirm our hypothesis.'
questions.explanation: En effet opens the sentence to justify the preceding claim.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Growth is slowing; employment, on the other hand, is rising.'
questions.explanation: En revanche marks the contrast between the two clauses joined by a semicolon.

activities.title: Défendre une position
activities.intro: You play 'Me'; argue a case using logical connectors.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Faut-il vraiment investir dans le télétravail ?
lines.en: Should we really invest in remote work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui. En effet, la productivité a augmenté depuis sa mise en place.
lines.en: Yes. Indeed, productivity has risen since it was introduced.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mais certains se sentent isolés.
lines.en: But some people feel isolated.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Néanmoins, on peut prévoir des journées au bureau.
lines.en: Nevertheless, we can plan days at the office.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et les coûts de bureau ?
lines.en: And the office costs?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Par ailleurs, ils diminuent ; en revanche, il faut investir dans le numérique.
lines.en: Moreover, they decrease; on the other hand, we must invest in digital tools.

days.title: La mise en relief & les registres de langue
days.objective: Emphasize elements with cleft structures and adapt between formal and informal registers.
days.paretoFocus: Use c'est...qui/que and ce qui...c'est to highlight, while shifting register appropriately.
drills: Rewrite 5 neutral sentences using c'est...qui or c'est...que.
Convert 3 informal sentences into a formal register.
Highlight the object of 3 sentences using ce que...c'est.

items: Recall the difference between c'est...qui and c'est...que.
State two markers of the familiar register to avoid at the DELF oral.
Write one cleft sentence emphasizing a time expression.

activities.title: Mettre en valeur et adapter le ton
activities.intro: Cleft sentences foreground a chosen element; register shifts change vocabulary and structure without changing meaning.

items.hr: C'est toi qui as raison.
items.en: You are the one who is right.
items.note: qui highlights the subject; verb agrees with toi

items.hr: C'est ce livre que je préfère.
items.en: This book is the one I prefer.

items.hr: Ce qui m'intéresse, c'est la culture.
items.en: What interests me is culture.

items.hr: Je ne comprends pas. (soutenu) / J'pige pas. (familier)
items.en: I do not understand. / I don't get it.

items.hr: Auriez-vous l'obligeance de m'aider ?
items.en: Would you be so kind as to help me?

activities.title: Emphase et registre
activities.intro: Build cleft structures and identify the appropriate register.

questions.prompt: Emphasize the subject: 'Marie a organisé la fête.'
options: C'est Marie qui a organisé la fête.
C'est Marie que a organisé la fête.
C'est Marie qu'a organisé la fête.
C'est à Marie qui a organisé la fête.
questions.answer: C'est Marie qui a organisé la fête.
questions.explanation: Qui highlights the subject in a cleft sentence: c'est Marie qui...

questions.prompt: Which sentence belongs to the formal register?
questions.explanation: The vous form with the conditional pourriez marks polite, formal register.

questions.prompt: Complete the cleft sentence emphasizing the object: 'C'est ce projet ___ nous soutenons.'
questions.explanation: Que introduces the highlighted direct object in a cleft sentence.

questions.prompt: Complete the cleft: '___ qui compte, c'est la régularité.' (fill the first two words)
questions.explanation: Ce qui...c'est is the standard structure to emphasize a subject idea.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'It was in 2015 that everything changed.'
questions.explanation: A time expression is emphasized with c'est...que.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'What I prefer is reading.'
questions.explanation: Ce que...c'est emphasizes the object of the verb préférer.

activities.title: Adapter son discours
activities.intro: You play 'Me'; emphasize your points and keep a formal register.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce qui vous a le plus marqué dans ce stage ?
lines.en: What struck you most during this internship?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ce qui m'a le plus marqué, c'est l'esprit d'équipe.
lines.en: What struck me most is the team spirit.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et une compétence clé ?
lines.en: And a key skill?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est la gestion du temps que j'ai vraiment développée.
lines.en: It is time management that I really developed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Recommanderiez-vous cette entreprise ?
lines.en: Would you recommend this company?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tout à fait ; c'est un environnement que je qualifierais d'exemplaire.
lines.en: Absolutely; it is an environment I would describe as exemplary.

days.title: L'argumentation et le débat de société
days.objective: Build a toolkit of connectors and rhetorical moves to structure a coherent B2 argument.
days.paretoFocus: Master the handful of logical connectors that carry 80% of argumentative cohesion in oral and written debate.
drills: Write a 120-word paragraph defending a thesis using at least four different logical connectors.
Record yourself introducing an argument, developing it, and illustrating it with one concrete example.
Rephrase five blunt opinions into nuanced statements using 'il me semble que' and 'on pourrait soutenir que'.

items: Recall five connectors that introduce a cause and five that introduce a consequence.
Transform 'Je pense que oui' into three more formal argumentative openings.
Say aloud a full argument: thesis, one reason, one example, one mini-conclusion.

activities.title: Les articulateurs de l'argumentation
activities.intro: These connectors let you signal cause, consequence, opposition and conclusion so your reasoning reads as structured, not listed.

items.hr: d'une part... d'autre part...
items.en: on the one hand... on the other hand...
items.note: Balances two facets of an argument.

items.hr: en effet
items.en: indeed / in fact
items.note: Introduces a justification, not a new idea.

items.hr: par conséquent
items.en: consequently

items.hr: force est de constater que
items.en: one is forced to admit that
items.note: Elegant way to concede an obvious fact.

items.hr: en définitive
items.en: ultimately / all things considered

activities.title: Structurer un raisonnement
activities.intro: Choose or supply the connector that makes each argumentative sentence coherent and formal.

questions.prompt: Which connector best introduces a justification: 'Ce projet est viable; ___, les chiffres le confirment.'
questions.explanation: 'En effet' confirms and justifies the preceding claim, unlike the contrastive options.

questions.prompt: Pick the marker of consequence: 'Les preuves manquent; ___ l'accusation s'effondre.'
questions.explanation: 'Par conséquent' expresses the logical result; the others express addition, opposition or concession.

questions.prompt: Type the formal expression meaning 'one is forced to admit that' (starts with 'force').
questions.explanation: A staple B2 concession opener before nuancing your point.

questions.prompt: Complete with a two-part balancing connector: 'D'une part le coût, ___ le bénéfice social.'
questions.explanation: 'D'une part... d'autre part...' balances two aspects of the same issue.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Ultimately, this position needs to be nuanced.'
questions.explanation: 'Il convient de' + infinitive is an impersonal, formal way to state what should be done.

activities.title: Un débat citoyen
activities.intro: You play 'Me' in a moderated debate; defend a position while acknowledging the other side.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Selon vous, faut-il rendre le vote obligatoire?
lines.en: In your view, should voting be made compulsory?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il me semble que oui, car l'abstention fragilise la légitimité démocratique.
lines.en: It seems to me that yes, because abstention weakens democratic legitimacy.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mais n'est-ce pas une atteinte à la liberté individuelle?
lines.en: But isn't that an infringement on individual freedom?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certes, l'objection est recevable; toutefois, voter est aussi un devoir civique.
lines.en: Admittedly, the objection is valid; however, voting is also a civic duty.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certains voteraient alors sans conviction.
lines.en: Some would then vote without conviction.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En effet, mais on pourrait prévoir un bulletin blanc reconnu, ce qui répond à cette crainte.
lines.en: Indeed, but one could introduce a recognised blank ballot, which answers that concern.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En définitive, je défends une participation renforcée, sans coercition brutale.
lines.en: Ultimately, I defend stronger participation, without brutal coercion.

days.title: Politique et institutions
days.objective: Acquire the institutional vocabulary needed to discuss governance, powers and democratic debate.
days.paretoFocus: Learn the core lexicon of the three powers and electoral life that recurs across DELF B2 political topics.
drills: Summarise in French the roles of the executive, legislative and judicial branches in three sentences.
Draft an opinion on decentralisation using at least three institutional terms.
Explain aloud the difference between 'un projet de loi' and 'une proposition de loi'.

items: Recall the French names of the three separated powers.
Give two synonyms for 'the government' at different registers.
State one argument for and one against proportional representation in French.

activities.title: Le vocabulaire des institutions
activities.intro: This lexicon lets you name institutions and processes precisely instead of relying on vague terms.

items.hr: le pouvoir exécutif
items.en: the executive power

items.hr: le pouvoir législatif
items.en: the legislative power

items.hr: un projet de loi
items.en: a government bill
items.note: Introduced by the government, unlike 'une proposition de loi' from MPs.

items.hr: le suffrage universel
items.en: universal suffrage

items.hr: l'alternance politique
items.en: the alternation of power
items.note: Change of governing majority through elections.

activities.title: Nommer les institutions
activities.intro: Test your precision with institutional terms and their correct use.

questions.prompt: A bill introduced by members of parliament is called:
questions.explanation: 'Proposition de loi' comes from parliamentarians; 'projet de loi' comes from the government.

questions.prompt: Which term names the change of governing majority after elections?
questions.explanation: 'L'alternance' is the peaceful handover of power to a new majority.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'the separation of powers'.
questions.explanation: A foundational concept of the rule of law, frequent in political debate.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Le président est élu au ___ universel direct.'
questions.explanation: 'Suffrage universel direct' means direct universal suffrage.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Decentralisation brings decisions closer to citizens.'
questions.explanation: 'Rapprocher X de Y' means to bring X closer to Y.

activities.title: Faut-il réformer les institutions?
activities.intro: You play 'Me', arguing about institutional reform with nuance.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pensez-vous qu'il faille introduire une dose de proportionnelle?
lines.en: Do you think a dose of proportional representation should be introduced?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, car cela garantirait une meilleure représentation des minorités politiques.
lines.en: Yes, because it would ensure better representation of political minorities.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mais la proportionnelle ne risque-t-elle pas l'instabilité gouvernementale?
lines.en: But doesn't proportional representation risk governmental instability?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est un risque réel; néanmoins, un seuil minimal limiterait la fragmentation.
lines.en: That is a real risk; nevertheless, a minimum threshold would limit fragmentation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Les électeurs comprendraient-ils un tel système?
lines.en: Would voters understand such a system?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Avec de la pédagogie civique, oui; d'ailleurs, plusieurs démocraties voisines le pratiquent.
lines.en: With civic education, yes; moreover, several neighbouring democracies practise it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En somme, je plaide pour un équilibre entre représentativité et gouvernabilité.
lines.en: In short, I argue for a balance between representativeness and governability.

days.title: Sciences, environnement et éthique
days.objective: Discuss scientific progress and ecological dilemmas using precise, evaluative vocabulary.
days.paretoFocus: Focus on the ethics-of-science and environment lexicon that dominates DELF B2 essay and debate prompts.
drills: Argue in French whether scientific progress should have ethical limits, in five sentences.
List five environmental problems and pair each with a proposed solution in French.
Explain the precautionary principle aloud in your own words.

items: Recall three collocations with 'développement' related to sustainability.
Give the French for 'greenhouse gas emissions'.
State one ethical objection to genetic engineering in French.

activities.title: Sciences et enjeux environnementaux
activities.intro: This vocabulary lets you frame ecological and ethical debates with the right technical register.

items.hr: le développement durable
items.en: sustainable development
items.note: Meets present needs without compromising future ones.

items.hr: le principe de précaution
items.en: the precautionary principle
items.note: Act cautiously when risks are uncertain.

items.hr: les émissions de gaz à effet de serre
items.en: greenhouse gas emissions

items.hr: les dérives de la science
items.en: the excesses of science

items.hr: remettre en cause
items.en: to call into question
items.note: Useful verb for challenging a consensus.

activities.title: L'éthique du progrès
activities.intro: Apply environmental and ethical vocabulary in argumentative contexts.

questions.prompt: Which term names acting cautiously when a risk is scientifically uncertain?
questions.explanation: 'Le principe de précaution' governs decisions under scientific uncertainty.

questions.prompt: Choose the best formal verb for 'to challenge a scientific consensus':
questions.explanation: 'Remettre en cause' means to call into question, ideal for debate.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'greenhouse gas emissions'.
questions.answer: les émissions de gaz à effet de serre
questions.explanation: A recurring collocation in environmental discourse.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Le ___ durable concilie économie, société et environnement.'
questions.explanation: 'Développement durable' is sustainable development.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Research must submit to ethical oversight.'
questions.explanation: 'Se soumettre à' means to submit to / comply with.

activities.title: Faut-il tout autoriser au nom du progrès?
activities.intro: You play 'Me', debating the ethical limits of science.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La science ne devrait connaître aucune limite, selon moi.
lines.en: Science should have no limits, in my opinion.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je comprends l'argument, mais toute avancée doit rester encadrée par l'éthique.
lines.en: I understand the argument, but every advance must remain governed by ethics.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: N'est-ce pas freiner l'innovation?
lines.en: Isn't that slowing down innovation?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas nécessairement; le principe de précaution oriente sans interdire.
lines.en: Not necessarily; the precautionary principle guides without forbidding.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourtant, les manipulations génétiques inquiètent le public.
lines.en: Yet genetic manipulations worry the public.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Justement, la transparence et un débat citoyen apaiseraient ces craintes.
lines.en: Precisely, transparency and public debate would ease those fears.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En définitive, je défends un progrès responsable plutôt qu'illimité.
lines.en: Ultimately, I defend responsible rather than unlimited progress.

days.title: Culture, médias et arts
days.objective: Talk critically about media, culture and the arts and evaluate their social role.
days.paretoFocus: Prioritise the media-literacy and cultural-value lexicon that anchors DELF B2 cultural debates.
drills: Write a short critique of a film or exhibition using three evaluative adjectives in French.
Argue whether public funding of culture is justified, in five sentences.
Explain aloud the difference between 'information' and 'désinformation'.

items: Recall three verbs for expressing a cultural judgement.
Give the French for 'fake news' and 'echo chamber'.
State one argument that culture should remain accessible to all in French.

activities.title: Médias, culture et esprit critique
activities.intro: These expressions let you evaluate cultural works and analyse the influence of the media.

items.hr: l'esprit critique
items.en: critical thinking
items.note: Ability to question sources and arguments.

items.hr: la désinformation
items.en: disinformation

items.hr: le pluralisme des médias
items.en: media pluralism

items.hr: démocratiser la culture
items.en: to make culture accessible to all

items.hr: une œuvre marquante
items.en: a landmark work

activities.title: Juger et analyser
activities.intro: Use cultural and media vocabulary to form precise evaluations.

questions.prompt: Which term names deliberately false information spread to mislead?
questions.explanation: 'La désinformation' is intentionally misleading information.

questions.prompt: Pick the phrase meaning 'to make culture accessible to all':
questions.explanation: 'Démocratiser la culture' means broadening access to cultural life.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'critical thinking' (two words).
questions.explanation: 'L'esprit critique' is essential for evaluating media.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Le ___ des médias garantit la diversité des opinions.'
questions.explanation: 'Le pluralisme des médias' protects democratic debate.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'This exhibition challenges our aesthetic certainties.'
questions.explanation: 'Remettre en question' expresses challenging an assumption.

activities.title: L'État doit-il financer la culture?
activities.intro: You play 'Me', debating public funding of the arts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Financer la culture avec l'argent public me semble discutable.
lines.en: Funding culture with public money seems questionable to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vous entends, mais la culture est un bien commun qui mérite protection.
lines.en: I hear you, but culture is a common good that deserves protection.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le marché ne pourrait-il pas suffire?
lines.en: Couldn't the market suffice?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En partie; toutefois, sans soutien public, les œuvres exigeantes disparaîtraient.
lines.en: In part; however, without public support, demanding works would disappear.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certains y voient un gaspillage.
lines.en: Some see it as a waste.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Au contraire, chaque euro investi démocratise l'accès au savoir et à la beauté.
lines.en: On the contrary, every euro invested widens access to knowledge and beauty.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En définitive, je défends un financement transparent et évalué, non aveugle.
lines.en: Ultimately, I defend transparent and evaluated funding, not blind funding.

days.title: Travail et économie
days.objective: Debate the transformations of work and the economy with accurate socioeconomic vocabulary.
days.paretoFocus: Concentrate on the labour-market and economic-policy lexicon central to DELF B2 work topics.
drills: Argue for or against the four-day week in five French sentences.
Describe three effects of automation on employment in French.
Explain aloud the difference between 'le chômage' and 'la précarité'.

items: Recall three collocations with 'marché du travail'.
Give the French for 'purchasing power' and 'job insecurity'.
State one argument that telework improves quality of life in French.

activities.title: Le monde du travail
activities.intro: This lexicon lets you analyse employment, inequality and economic policy with precision.

items.hr: le marché du travail
items.en: the labour market

items.hr: le pouvoir d'achat
items.en: purchasing power

items.hr: la précarité
items.en: job insecurity / precariousness

items.hr: concilier vie professionnelle et vie privée
items.en: to balance work and private life

items.hr: creuser les inégalités
items.en: to widen inequalities

activities.title: Analyser l'économie du travail
activities.intro: Deploy economic vocabulary in argumentative sentences about work.

questions.prompt: Which term names unstable, uncertain employment?
questions.explanation: 'La précarité' describes insecure working conditions.

questions.prompt: Choose the verb phrase meaning 'to widen inequalities':
questions.explanation: 'Creuser' literally digs deeper, hence widening gaps.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'purchasing power'.
questions.explanation: 'Le pouvoir d'achat' is a frequent economic term.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Le télétravail aide à ___ vie professionnelle et vie privée.'
questions.explanation: 'Concilier' means to reconcile or balance two demands.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Automation threatens some low-skilled jobs.'
questions.explanation: 'Peu qualifié' means low-skilled; word order follows the noun.

activities.title: La semaine de quatre jours est-elle souhaitable?
activities.intro: You play 'Me', debating the four-day week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Travailler quatre jours nuirait à la compétitivité, non?
lines.en: Working four days would harm competitiveness, wouldn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas forcément; plusieurs études montrent une productivité maintenue, voire accrue.
lines.en: Not necessarily; several studies show productivity maintained, or even increased.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mais toutes les entreprises ne peuvent l'appliquer.
lines.en: But not all companies can apply it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est juste; il faudrait donc une mise en œuvre sectorielle et progressive.
lines.en: That's fair; so it would require a sector-based and gradual rollout.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le pouvoir d'achat des salariés?
lines.en: And employees' purchasing power?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il serait préservé si la réforme se fait à salaire constant.
lines.en: It would be preserved if the reform keeps wages unchanged.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En somme, je vois là un levier de bien-être et d'efficacité, sous conditions.
lines.en: In short, I see it as a lever for well-being and efficiency, under certain conditions.

days.title: La mondialisation
days.objective: Weigh the benefits and drawbacks of globalisation using balanced argumentative language.
days.paretoFocus: Learn the globalisation lexicon and the pros/cons framing that DELF B2 debate prompts demand.
drills: List three advantages and three drawbacks of globalisation in French.
Argue whether globalisation threatens cultural diversity, in five sentences.
Explain aloud the difference between 'libre-échange' and 'protectionnisme'.

items: Recall three collocations with 'échanges' related to trade.
Give the French for 'relocation of production' and 'offshoring'.
State one argument that globalisation reduces poverty in French.

activities.title: Les enjeux de la mondialisation
activities.intro: This vocabulary lets you name economic and cultural flows and evaluate their consequences.

items.hr: le libre-échange
items.en: free trade

items.hr: les délocalisations
items.en: offshoring / relocations

items.hr: l'uniformisation culturelle
items.en: cultural homogenisation

items.hr: les inégalités Nord-Sud
items.en: North-South inequalities
items.note: Gaps between rich and poor regions.

items.hr: tirer parti de
items.en: to take advantage of

activities.title: Peser le pour et le contre
activities.intro: Use globalisation vocabulary to build balanced arguments.

questions.prompt: Which term names moving production abroad to cut costs?
questions.explanation: 'Les délocalisations' means relocating production overseas.

questions.prompt: Choose the term for trade without tariff barriers:
questions.explanation: 'Le libre-échange' is free trade, opposed to protectionism.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'cultural homogenisation'.
questions.explanation: Often cited as a cultural risk of globalisation.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Les pays émergents cherchent à ___ parti de la mondialisation.'
questions.explanation: 'Tirer parti de' means to take advantage of.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Globalisation heightens both wealth and disparities.'
questions.explanation: 'À la fois... et...' balances two simultaneous effects.

activities.title: La mondialisation menace-t-elle les cultures locales?
activities.intro: You play 'Me', debating globalisation and cultural diversity.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La mondialisation efface peu à peu les identités locales.
lines.en: Globalisation gradually erases local identities.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le risque existe, mais elle favorise aussi le métissage et les échanges.
lines.en: The risk exists, but it also fosters cultural blending and exchange.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourtant, les mêmes marques dominent partout.
lines.en: Yet the same brands dominate everywhere.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certes; toutefois, les cultures locales se réinventent souvent face à cette pression.
lines.en: Admittedly; however, local cultures often reinvent themselves under that pressure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: N'est-ce pas une résistance minoritaire?
lines.en: Isn't that a minority resistance?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Peut-être, mais les politiques culturelles peuvent en faire une force durable.
lines.en: Perhaps, but cultural policies can turn it into a lasting strength.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En définitive, je crois à une mondialisation qui enrichit sans uniformiser.
lines.en: Ultimately, I believe in a globalisation that enriches without homogenising.

days.title: Défendre une opinion nuancée : concession et réfutation
days.objective: Concede an opposing point and refute it while defending a nuanced overall position.
days.paretoFocus: Drill the concession-then-refutation structures that distinguish a B2 argument from a flat opinion.
drills: Write three sentences that concede a point then refute it using 'certes... mais...'.
Turn a categorical claim into a nuanced one using 'dans une certaine mesure'.
Practise refuting an argument aloud with 'il n'en demeure pas moins que'.

items: Recall three concession connectors and three refutation connectors.
Transform 'Vous avez tort' into a polite, formal refutation.
Say aloud a full concession-refutation move on any topic.

activities.title: Concéder pour mieux réfuter
activities.intro: These structures let you acknowledge the opponent before dismantling their argument, the hallmark of nuance.

items.hr: certes... mais...
items.en: admittedly... but...

items.hr: il n'en demeure pas moins que
items.en: the fact remains that
items.note: Reasserts your point after a concession.

items.hr: dans une certaine mesure
items.en: to a certain extent

items.hr: loin de moi l'idée de
items.en: far be it from me to
items.note: Polite way to reject an interpretation.

items.hr: on aurait tort de croire que
items.en: one would be wrong to believe that

activities.title: Nuancer et réfuter
activities.intro: Practise the grammar of concession and rebuttal in formal French.

questions.prompt: Which phrase reasserts your point after conceding: 'C'est coûteux; ___ le projet reste indispensable.'
options: il n'en demeure pas moins que
questions.answer: il n'en demeure pas moins que
questions.explanation: It concedes the cost yet firmly maintains the conclusion.

questions.prompt: Choose the partial-agreement marker:
questions.explanation: 'Dans une certaine mesure' signals qualified, not total, agreement.

questions.prompt: Type the concession pair meaning 'admittedly... but...' (three words with an ellipsis, e.g. 'x... y...').
questions.explanation: The most common B2 concession-refutation structure.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite rejection: 'Loin de moi l'idée de ___ votre sincérité.'
questions.explanation: 'Contester' means to dispute; the phrase softens disagreement.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'It would be wrong to believe the question is simple.'
questions.explanation: 'On aurait tort de croire que' is a refined refutation opener.

activities.title: Un désaccord courtois
activities.intro: You play 'Me', disagreeing with nuance while conceding valid points.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Les réseaux sociaux sont responsables de la désinformation.
lines.en: Social networks are responsible for disinformation.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certes, ils l'amplifient; mais ils ne l'ont pas inventée pour autant.
lines.en: Admittedly, they amplify it; but they did not invent it, either.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous minimisez leur rôle, il me semble.
lines.en: You are downplaying their role, it seems to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Loin de moi l'idée de les excuser; il n'en demeure pas moins que l'éducation compte davantage.
lines.en: Far be it from me to excuse them; the fact remains that education matters more.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: L'éducation suffirait-elle vraiment?
lines.en: Would education really suffice?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dans une certaine mesure, oui, si elle est couplée à une régulation intelligente.
lines.en: To a certain extent, yes, if it is combined with smart regulation.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En somme, je refuse d'accabler un seul acteur d'un problème collectif.
lines.en: In short, I refuse to blame a single actor for a collective problem.

days.title: La synthèse de documents
days.objective: Confront several documents and produce a coherent, objective synthesis without personal opinion.
days.paretoFocus: Master the reformulation and cross-referencing phrases that structure the DELF B2 synthesis task.
drills: Reformulate a document's main idea in French without copying its wording.
Write two sentences that cross-reference two sources with 'tandis que' and 'de même'.
Draft a neutral synthesis introduction announcing the common theme in French.

items: Recall three verbs for reporting a source's viewpoint neutrally.
Give the French for 'to cross-reference' and 'to reformulate'.
State the rule about personal opinion in a synthesis, in French.

activities.title: Confronter et reformuler les documents
activities.intro: These phrases let you attribute, compare and reformulate ideas objectively, as the synthesis exercise requires.

items.hr: selon l'auteur
items.en: according to the author
items.note: Attributes an idea without endorsing it.

items.hr: mettre en parallèle
items.en: to cross-reference / juxtapose

items.hr: reformuler avec ses propres mots
items.en: to reformulate in one's own words

items.hr: les deux documents convergent sur
items.en: both documents agree on

items.hr: en revanche
items.en: on the other hand

activities.title: Rédiger une synthèse objective
activities.intro: Practise the neutral, attributive language of the synthesis exercise.

questions.prompt: Which phrase best signals divergence between two documents?
questions.explanation: 'En revanche' contrasts two sources; the others add or illustrate.

questions.prompt: In a synthesis, personal opinion should be:
questions.explanation: A synthesis is objective; the writer's own opinion must be absent.

questions.prompt: Type the French verb phrase for 'to cross-reference / juxtapose' documents (starts with 'mettre').
questions.explanation: 'Mettre en parallèle' compares sources side by side.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Les deux textes ___ sur la nécessité d'agir vite.'
questions.explanation: 'Converger sur' means to agree on a shared point.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Ideas should be rephrased without copying them.'
questions.explanation: 'Sans + infinitif' expresses the manner: without copying them.

activities.title: Préparer une synthèse à deux
activities.intro: You play 'Me', coaching a partner through the method of the synthesis.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Par où commencer pour rédiger une synthèse?
lines.en: Where should I start to write a synthesis?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'abord, dégagez le thème commun aux documents, puis leur problématique.
lines.en: First, identify the theme common to the documents, then their central question.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Puis-je donner mon avis ensuite?
lines.en: Can I give my opinion afterwards?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Surtout pas; votre opinion doit rester absente, seule compte la confrontation des idées.
lines.en: Absolutely not; your opinion must remain absent, only the confrontation of ideas matters.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment relier les sources entre elles?
lines.en: How do I connect the sources to each other?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mettez-les en parallèle: montrez où elles convergent et, en revanche, où elles divergent.
lines.en: Cross-reference them: show where they agree and, on the other hand, where they differ.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Enfin, reformulez tout avec vos propres mots pour éviter le plagiat.
lines.en: Finally, reformulate everything in your own words to avoid plagiarism.

days.title: Compréhension de l'oral B2 : stratégies et pratique
days.objective: Master active-listening strategies to score high on the DELF B2 listening section.
days.paretoFocus: Anticipate content from the title and questions before the audio starts.
drills: Listen to a 3-minute French radio extract and note the main idea in one sentence
Identify three connecteurs logiques signalling opinion or contrast in an audio clip
Write down five keywords predicted from the questions before listening

items: Recall the two audio formats the B2 listening paper uses
Re-listen to yesterday's clip and check your keyword predictions
Summarise one news report orally in 30 seconds

activities.title: Décoder l'audio B2
activities.intro: Key strategies and expressions for the listening comprehension.

items.hr: Repérer les mots-clés avant l'écoute
items.en: Spot the keywords before listening

items.hr: D'après le journaliste, il s'agirait d'un problème structurel.
items.en: According to the journalist, it would be a structural problem.

items.hr: L'intervenant met l'accent sur les conséquences économiques.
items.en: The speaker emphasises the economic consequences.

items.hr: Autrement dit, la mesure serait inefficace.
items.en: In other words, the measure would be ineffective.

items.hr: Prendre des notes en abrégé pour ne pas perdre le fil
items.en: Take shorthand notes so as not to lose the thread

activities.title: Écoute stratégique
activities.intro: Test your listening strategy vocabulary and comprehension.

questions.prompt: What should you do FIRST when the listening section begins?
options: Read the questions to anticipate content
questions.answer: Read the questions to anticipate content
questions.explanation: Reading the questions primes you to catch the relevant information during the audio.

questions.prompt: Which grammatical form usually signals unverified or reported information in a news report?
questions.explanation: The conditionnel (e.g. 'il s'agirait') marks information the speaker cannot confirm.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Type the connector meaning "in other words" used to reformulate.'
questions.explanation: 'Autrement dit' introduces a reformulation of a previous idea.

questions.prompt: Type the French verb phrase meaning 'to emphasise' (mettre l'... sur).
questions.explanation: 'Mettre l'accent sur' means to stress or emphasise a point.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The report tackles the issue of global warming.'
questions.explanation: 'Aborder une question' means to tackle a topic; word order follows subject-verb-object.

activities.title: Après l'écoute : vérifier sa compréhension
activities.intro: Play 'Me' (the candidate) discussing a listening extract with a study partner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: As-tu saisi l'idée principale du reportage ?
lines.en: Did you grasp the main idea of the report?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, il s'agissait des effets de la pollution sur la santé publique.
lines.en: Yes, it was about the effects of pollution on public health.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et quel était le point de vue du spécialiste interrogé ?
lines.en: And what was the viewpoint of the expert interviewed?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il mettait l'accent sur l'urgence d'agir, tout en restant prudent.
lines.en: He emphasised the urgency of acting, while remaining cautious.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment as-tu repéré les informations non confirmées ?
lines.en: How did you spot the unconfirmed information?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Grâce au conditionnel : le journaliste disait que la mesure 'serait' contestée.
lines.en: Thanks to the conditional: the journalist said the measure 'would be' contested.

days.title: Compréhension des écrits B2 : le texte argumentatif
days.objective: Analyse argumentative texts to identify thesis, arguments and the author's stance.
days.paretoFocus: Distinguish the author's thesis from the counter-arguments they cite.
drills: Underline the thesis sentence in an opinion article
List the connecteurs d'opposition found in a two-page text
Rephrase the author's main argument in one French sentence

items: Recall three connecteurs de concession
Find the implicit conclusion in a short editorial
Explain the difference between thèse and antithèse aloud

activities.title: Anatomie d'un texte argumentatif
activities.intro: Structural vocabulary for reading argumentative texts.

items.hr: La thèse défendue par l'auteur
items.en: The thesis defended by the author

items.hr: Certes, cet argument semble valable, mais il ignore un point essentiel.
items.en: Admittedly, this argument seems valid, but it ignores an essential point.

items.hr: L'auteur réfute l'idée selon laquelle la technologie nuit au lien social.
items.en: The author refutes the idea that technology harms social bonds.

items.hr: En définitive, l'auteur plaide en faveur d'une réforme.
items.en: Ultimately, the author argues in favour of a reform.

items.hr: Un argument étayé par des exemples concrets
items.en: An argument backed by concrete examples

activities.title: Lire pour argumenter
activities.intro: Practice identifying argumentative structures.

questions.prompt: In an argumentative text, what is the 'thèse'?
options: The main position the author defends
questions.answer: The main position the author defends
questions.explanation: The thèse is the central claim the author sets out to support.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces a concession before a rebuttal?
questions.explanation: 'Certes... mais' concedes a point then counters it, a hallmark of argumentation.

questions.prompt: Type the French verb meaning 'to refute/disprove' an idea.
questions.explanation: 'Réfuter' means to argue against and reject an idea.

questions.prompt: Type the French phrase for 'to argue in favour of' (plaider en...).
questions.explanation: 'Plaider en faveur de' expresses supporting a cause or position.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The author defends a solidly argued thesis.'
questions.explanation: The adverb 'solidement' precedes the past participle 'argumentée' acting as adjective.

activities.title: Débattre d'un article
activities.intro: Play 'Me' (the candidate) analysing an editorial with a partner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quelle est la thèse principale de cet éditorial ?
lines.en: What is the main thesis of this editorial?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'auteur soutient que le télétravail améliore la qualité de vie.
lines.en: The author maintains that remote work improves quality of life.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Évoque-t-il des objections à cette idée ?
lines.en: Does he mention objections to this idea?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, il concède que l'isolement peut poser problème, mais il le relativise.
lines.en: Yes, he concedes that isolation can be an issue, but he plays it down.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment étaye-t-il son propos ?
lines.en: How does he support his point?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il s'appuie sur des études récentes et des témoignages de salariés.
lines.en: He relies on recent studies and employee testimonials.

days.title: Production écrite : le texte argumenté (250 mots), structure et connecteurs
days.objective: Build a well-structured 250-word argumentative essay with clear connectors.
days.paretoFocus: Master an intro-development-conclusion plan with signposting connectors.
drills: Write an introduction with an accroche and an announced plan
Draft two developed paragraphs each with one argument and one example
Write a conclusion that restates your position and opens the debate

items: Recall five connecteurs d'articulation for an essay
Count the words in a sample paragraph to gauge the 250-word target
Rewrite one weak sentence into a more formal register

activities.title: Charpente de l'essai argumenté
activities.intro: Connectors and formulas to structure a B2 essay.

items.hr: Tout d'abord, il convient de souligner que...
items.en: First of all, it should be stressed that...

items.hr: De plus, cette idée se vérifie dans les faits.
items.en: Moreover, this idea is borne out by the facts.

items.hr: En revanche, certains y voient un inconvénient majeur.
items.en: On the other hand, some see a major drawback in it.

items.hr: Pour conclure, je suis convaincu(e) que cette évolution est bénéfique.
items.en: To conclude, I am convinced this development is beneficial.

items.hr: Prendre position tout en nuançant son propos
items.en: Take a stance while qualifying one's point

activities.title: Connecteurs et structure
activities.intro: Practice the building blocks of the argumentative essay.

questions.prompt: Which connector best introduces a contrasting argument?
questions.explanation: 'En revanche' signals opposition between two ideas.

questions.prompt: What should the introduction of a B2 essay contain?
options: An accroche and the announced plan
questions.answer: An accroche and the announced plan
questions.explanation: A B2 introduction hooks the reader and announces how the essay will unfold.

questions.prompt: Type the French connector meaning 'moreover/furthermore' (de + noun).
questions.explanation: 'De plus' adds a supporting argument.

questions.prompt: Type the French phrase 'to conclude' used to open a conclusion.
questions.explanation: 'Pour conclure' signals the closing paragraph of the essay.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Ultimately this measure seems justified to me.'
questions.explanation: 'En définitive' opens a concluding statement; 'me semble' expresses a nuanced opinion.

activities.title: Planifier son essai
activities.intro: Play 'Me' (the candidate) planning an essay with a tutor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quel plan comptes-tu adopter pour ce sujet ?
lines.en: What plan do you intend to adopt for this topic?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un plan dialectique : thèse, antithèse, puis synthèse.
lines.en: A dialectical plan: thesis, antithesis, then synthesis.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment vas-tu introduire ton premier argument ?
lines.en: How will you introduce your first argument?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tout d'abord, je soulignerai les avantages économiques.
lines.en: First of all, I will stress the economic advantages.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: N'oublie pas de nuancer ta position.
lines.en: Don't forget to qualify your position.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr, j'apporterai une concession avant de conclure fermement.
lines.en: Of course, I'll offer a concession before concluding firmly.

days.title: Production orale : défendre un point de vue à partir d'un document
days.objective: Deliver a structured spoken argument based on a short trigger document.
days.paretoFocus: Turn a document's theme into a clear thesis and two supporting arguments.
drills: Read a short article and formulate a thesis in one spoken sentence
Improvise a two-minute monologue defending that thesis
Prepare two arguments with a linking connector for each

items: Recall three expressions for giving a personal opinion
Time a one-minute spoken introduction
List two ways to buy thinking time politely

activities.title: Prendre la parole avec assurance
activities.intro: Expressions to present and defend an opinion orally.

items.hr: Le document que j'ai choisi traite de...
items.en: The document I have chosen deals with...

items.hr: À mon sens, cette question mérite d'être nuancée.
items.en: In my view, this question deserves to be qualified.

items.hr: Je suis persuadé(e) que les bénéfices l'emportent sur les risques.
items.en: I am convinced the benefits outweigh the risks.

items.hr: Si je puis me permettre, j'ajouterais un dernier point.
items.en: If I may, I would add a final point.

items.hr: Pour illustrer mon propos, prenons l'exemple de...
items.en: To illustrate my point, let us take the example of...

activities.title: Structurer son monologue
activities.intro: Practice the language of the spoken presentation.

questions.prompt: How should you begin the monologue suivi in the production orale?
options: By presenting the document and its theme
By reading the whole document aloud
questions.answer: By presenting the document and its theme
questions.explanation: You introduce the document's source and topic, then announce your position.

questions.prompt: Which expression politely introduces a nuanced opinion?
questions.explanation: 'À mon sens' introduces a measured personal opinion, suitable for B2.

questions.prompt: Type the French phrase 'to illustrate my point' (pour ... mon propos).
questions.explanation: 'Pour illustrer mon propos' introduces a supporting example.

questions.prompt: Type the French phrase meaning 'I am convinced that' (je suis ... que).
questions.explanation: 'Je suis persuadé(e) que' strongly asserts a conviction.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'This document raises an important social issue.'
questions.explanation: 'Soulever une question' means to raise an issue; the adjective follows the noun phrase.

activities.title: Présenter son point de vue
activities.intro: Play 'Me' (the candidate) presenting a document to the examiner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pouvez-vous nous présenter le document que vous avez tiré au sort ?
lines.en: Can you present the document you drew?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le document que j'ai choisi traite de la place des écrans chez les jeunes.
lines.en: The document I chose deals with the place of screens among young people.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quel est votre point de vue sur ce sujet ?
lines.en: What is your viewpoint on this subject?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À mon sens, un usage encadré est préférable à une interdiction totale.
lines.en: In my view, supervised use is preferable to a total ban.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sur quoi vous appuyez-vous pour l'affirmer ?
lines.en: What do you base that claim on?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pour illustrer mon propos, je m'appuie sur mon expérience et des études récentes.
lines.en: To illustrate my point, I draw on my experience and recent studies.

days.title: L'exposé et le débat avec l'examinateur
days.objective: Sustain a debate with the examiner, defending your position under questioning.
days.paretoFocus: React to counter-arguments while staying courteous and coherent.
drills: Respond to three challenging objections without losing your thesis
Reformulate a point you made when the examiner misunderstands
Concede a valid point then redirect to your main argument

items: Recall three phrases to disagree politely
Practise asking for clarification formally
Rephrase one argument in two different ways

activities.title: Tenir un débat courtois
activities.intro: Expressions for the interactive debate phase.

items.hr: Je comprends votre objection, néanmoins je maintiens mon point de vue.
items.en: I understand your objection, nevertheless I maintain my viewpoint.

items.hr: Permettez-moi de préciser ma pensée.
items.en: Allow me to clarify my thinking.

items.hr: Vous soulevez un point intéressant, mais il faut le nuancer.
items.en: You raise an interesting point, but it needs qualifying.

items.hr: Loin de moi l'idée de nier ce problème.
items.en: Far be it from me to deny this problem.

items.hr: Si je vous suis bien, vous voulez dire que...
items.en: If I follow you correctly, you mean that...

activities.title: Répondre à la contradiction
activities.intro: Practice debate strategies and polite disagreement.

questions.prompt: How should you respond when the examiner challenges your position?
options: Acknowledge the objection, then defend your view
questions.answer: Acknowledge the objection, then defend your view
questions.explanation: B2 debate requires engaging with objections while sustaining your argument.

questions.prompt: Which phrase politely signals disagreement while conceding?
questions.explanation: It concedes a point ('certes') then introduces a qualification, staying courteous.

questions.prompt: Type the French connector meaning 'nevertheless' (one word).
questions.explanation: 'Néanmoins' introduces a strong but polite contrast.

questions.prompt: Type the French phrase 'allow me to clarify' (permettez-moi de ...).
questions.explanation: 'Permettez-moi de préciser' politely introduces a clarification.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I stand by my position despite your remark.'
questions.explanation: 'Malgré' + noun expresses concession while asserting one's stance.

activities.title: Le débat avec l'examinateur
activities.intro: Play 'Me' (the candidate) debating your thesis with the examiner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ne pensez-vous pas que votre position est un peu idéaliste ?
lines.en: Don't you think your position is a little idealistic?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je comprends votre objection, néanmoins les faits me donnent raison.
lines.en: I understand your objection, nevertheless the facts prove me right.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourtant, le coût d'une telle mesure serait considérable.
lines.en: Yet the cost of such a measure would be considerable.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certes, mais je nuancerais : cet investissement serait vite rentabilisé.
lines.en: Admittedly, but I would qualify that: the investment would soon pay off.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sur quels éléments fondez-vous cet optimisme ?
lines.en: On what do you base this optimism?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Permettez-moi de préciser : plusieurs pays voisins l'ont déjà démontré.
lines.en: Allow me to clarify: several neighbouring countries have already shown it.

days.title: Examen blanc DELF B2, compréhension (oral et écrit)
days.objective: Complete a timed mock of the listening and reading sections under exam conditions.
days.paretoFocus: Manage time strictly: pace reading to leave minutes for review.
drills: Do the listening section in one sitting with only two playbacks
Read two texts and answer within 40 minutes
Review all answers and flag two recurring error types

items: Recall the total duration of the compréhension sections
Re-read one text and correct a wrong answer
Note one listening trap you fell for and how to avoid it

activities.title: Consignes de l'examen blanc
activities.intro: Format reminders and exam-condition tips before the mock.

items.hr: La compréhension de l'oral dure environ 30 minutes.
items.en: The listening comprehension lasts about 30 minutes.

items.hr: La compréhension des écrits dure une heure.
items.en: The reading comprehension lasts one hour.

items.hr: Lisez toutes les consignes avant de répondre.
items.en: Read all the instructions before answering.

items.hr: Justifiez vos réponses en citant le texte quand c'est demandé.
items.en: Justify your answers by quoting the text when asked.

items.hr: Ne laissez aucune question sans réponse.
items.en: Leave no question unanswered.

activities.title: Simulation compréhension
activities.intro: Mock questions mirroring the DELF B2 comprehension tasks.

questions.prompt: How long does the DELF B2 compréhension des écrits last?
questions.explanation: The reading section is allotted one hour on the DELF B2.

questions.prompt: In a mock listening, you miss a detail on the first play. What is best?
options: Note the gap and catch it on the second play
Rewind mentally and miss the next part
questions.answer: Note the gap and catch it on the second play
questions.explanation: You typically get two listens; mark the gap and target it the second time.

questions.prompt: Type the French verb meaning 'to justify' your answer (infinitive).
questions.explanation: 'Justifier' sa réponse means to support your answer with evidence from the text.

questions.prompt: Type the French noun for 'the instructions/guidelines' (plural) on an exam.
questions.explanation: 'Les consignes' are the task instructions you must read carefully.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Justify your answer by quoting the text.'
questions.explanation: 'En citant' (gerund) expresses the means: by quoting the text.

activities.title: Débriefing de l'examinateur
activities.intro: Play 'Me' (the candidate) reviewing your mock comprehension with the examiner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment avez-vous géré votre temps sur la compréhension écrite ?
lines.en: How did you manage your time on the reading comprehension?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai consacré vingt minutes à chaque texte, puis dix minutes à la relecture.
lines.en: I spent twenty minutes on each text, then ten minutes reviewing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quelle a été la question la plus difficile à l'oral ?
lines.en: Which was the hardest question in the listening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Une question sur l'implicite : le ton ironique m'avait échappé.
lines.en: A question on implied meaning: the ironic tone had escaped me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment comptez-vous corriger cela ?
lines.en: How do you plan to correct that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En écoutant davantage d'émissions où le ton et l'ironie sont marqués.
lines.en: By listening to more programmes where tone and irony are marked.

days.title: Examen blanc DELF B2, production (écrite et orale)
days.objective: Complete a timed mock of the written and oral production sections.
days.paretoFocus: Respect the 250-word target and the oral prep-then-present timing.
drills: Write a 250-word argumentative essay in 60 minutes
Prepare a document in 30 minutes then present it in 20
Self-assess your essay against the DELF B2 criteria grid

items: Recall the word count expected for the production écrite
Correct three grammar errors in your mock essay
Re-record a 90-second improved oral introduction
