lines.en: I turned down the job abroad, in the end.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ah ? À ta place, j'aurais accepté sans hésiter.
lines.en: Oh? In your place, I would have accepted without hesitation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Peut-être, mais tu aurais dû voir les conditions de logement.
lines.en: Maybe, but you should have seen the housing conditions.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu aurais quand même pu négocier une prime.
lines.en: You could still have negotiated a bonus.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'aurais aimé, mais ils ne voulaient rien entendre.
lines.en: I would have liked to, but they would not hear of it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dommage. Avec un peu plus de temps, tu aurais sûrement obtenu mieux.
lines.en: Too bad. With a bit more time, you would surely have got something better.

days.title: Le passif complet : par ou de, se faire + infinitif
days.objective: You can choose between par and de for the passive agent, and use se faire + infinitive and impersonal passives to vary the passive voice.
days.paretoFocus: The shipped course teaches the basic passive; B2 writing needs its full range, including verbs of feeling that take de and the se faire construction that puts the victim in subject position.
drills: Turn two active sentences into the passive, choosing par or de for the agent.
Describe an incident with 'se faire' plus an infinitive, putting the victim as the subject.
Write one impersonal passive beginning 'Il a été décidé que'.

items: When is the passive agent introduced by de rather than par?
What does 'se faire + infinitive' add compared with a plain passive?
Does the participle agree in the passive, and with what?

activities.title: Varier la voix passive
activities.intro: The passive agent is normally introduced by par for an action, but by de after verbs of feeling or description (respecté de, entouré de). Se faire or se laisser plus an infinitive gives a passive sense with the affected person as subject. Impersonal passives (il a été décidé que) avoid naming an agent.

items.hr: Le voleur a été arrêté par la police.
items.en: The thief was arrested by the police.
items.note: par introduces the agent of an action

items.hr: Ce directeur est craint de ses employés.
items.en: This director is feared by his employees.
items.note: verbs of feeling take de for the agent

items.hr: La maison était entourée d'un grand jardin.
items.en: The house was surrounded by a large garden.

items.hr: Il s'est fait voler son téléphone dans le métro.
items.en: He had his phone stolen in the metro.
items.note: se faire + infinitive, the victim as subject; fait stays invariable

items.hr: Il a été décidé que la réunion serait reportée.
items.en: It was decided that the meeting would be postponed.

activities.title: Par, de et se faire
activities.intro: Practise the passive agent, the se faire construction and participle agreement.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct agent preposition: 'Ce directeur est craint ____ ses employés.'
questions.explanation: Verbs of feeling such as craindre introduce the agent with de.

questions.prompt: Turn to the passive: 'On a annulé le concert.' becomes:
questions.explanation: The passive is être in the right tense plus the participle agreeing with the subject.

questions.prompt: se faire + infinitive gives a passive meaning; fill the past participle of faire: Elle s'est ____ renverser par un vélo.
questions.explanation: In se faire + infinitive, fait stays invariable: elle s'est fait renverser.

questions.prompt: Agree the participle in the passive: Les décisions ont été ____ (prendre) hier. (feminine plural)
questions.explanation: In the passive the participle agrees with the subject décisions: prises.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The thief was arrested by the police.'
questions.explanation: The action passive uses par for the agent: par la police.

activities.title: Un vol dans le train
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You report a theft to a friend, using the passive and the se faire construction.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce qui t'est arrivé hier ? Tu as l'air contrarié.
lines.en: What happened to you yesterday? You look upset.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je me suis fait voler mon sac dans le train.
lines.en: I had my bag stolen on the train.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est affreux ! Le voleur a été retrouvé ?
lines.en: That is awful! Has the thief been found?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, mais il a été filmé par une caméra, paraît-il.
lines.en: No, but he was filmed by a camera, apparently.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Au moins la plainte a été enregistrée rapidement ?
lines.en: At least the complaint was registered quickly?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui. Il a été décidé que je serais recontacté sous huit jours.
lines.en: Yes. It was decided that I would be contacted again within a week.

days.title: Le ne explétif dans la phrase soutenue
days.objective: You can recognise and use the expletive ne after avant que, à moins que, craindre que, éviter que and comparatives, without turning the clause negative.
days.paretoFocus: Careful written French inserts an expletive ne that looks like a negation but is not; reading it as 'not' misreads the meaning, so B2 candidates must recognise it.
drills: Read three sentences with expletive ne aloud and confirm each is affirmative, not negative.
Write one sentence with 'avant que' and the expletive ne.
Write one comparative sentence ('plus... que') that inserts the expletive ne.

items: Does the expletive ne make the clause negative?
After which connectors here does the expletive ne appear?
How do you tell an expletive ne from a real negation?

activities.title: Un ne qui ne nie pas
activities.intro: In careful style an expletive ne appears without pas and does not negate the verb. You meet it after à moins que and de peur que, after avant que, with verbs of fearing or preventing such as craindre que and empêcher que and éviter que, and after comparatives of inequality. It belongs to formal register and can always be dropped in speech.

items.hr: Partons avant qu'il ne pleuve.
items.en: Let us leave before it rains.
items.note: avant que + expletive ne, no pas, not negative

items.hr: Je crains qu'il ne soit trop tard.
items.en: I fear it may be too late.

items.hr: Il est plus riche qu'il ne le laisse paraître.
items.en: He is richer than he lets on.

items.hr: Nous éviterons qu'une telle erreur ne se reproduise.
items.en: We will prevent such a mistake from happening again.

items.hr: À moins que tu ne changes d'avis, je pars seul.
items.en: Unless you change your mind, I am leaving alone.

activities.title: Reconnaître le ne explétif
activities.intro: Practise recognising and producing the expletive ne in formal clauses.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the expletive ne correctly (no negation intended)?
options: Je pars avant qu'il ne pleuve.
Je pars avant qu'il ne pleuve pas.
Je pars avant qu'il pleuve pas.
Je ne pars avant qu'il pleuve.
questions.answer: Je pars avant qu'il ne pleuve.
questions.explanation: After avant que the ne stands alone, without pas, and does not negate the verb.

questions.prompt: Complete the comparison with the right verb form: C'est plus difficile que je ne le ____.
questions.explanation: After a comparative of inequality careful French inserts ne; the verb here is the imperfect croyais.

questions.prompt: After a comparative, careful style adds the expletive particle before the verb. Complete: Il est plus riche qu'il ne le ____ paraître. (laisser, present)
questions.explanation: The expletive ne precedes the verb laisse; the clause stays affirmative.

questions.prompt: avant que triggers the subjunctive. Give the subjunctive of partir: Dis-lui au revoir avant qu'il ne ____.
questions.explanation: avant que takes the subjunctive parte, alongside the expletive ne.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Let us leave before it rains.'
questions.explanation: avant que takes the subjunctive pleuve with an expletive ne, no pas.

activities.title: Une randonnée risquée
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You plan a hike around a risk, using the expletive ne in careful speech.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On devrait peut-être reporter la randonnée.
lines.en: Maybe we should postpone the hike.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, partons tôt avant qu'il ne fasse trop chaud.
lines.en: Yes, let us leave early before it gets too hot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je crains que le sentier ne soit glissant après la pluie.
lines.en: I fear the path may be slippery after the rain.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prenons de bonnes chaussures, à moins que tu ne préfères annuler.
lines.en: Let us take good shoes, unless you would rather cancel.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Non, allons-y. C'est moins risqué que tu ne le penses.
lines.en: No, let us go. It is less risky than you think.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord. Prévenons les autres, pour éviter qu'ils ne s'inquiètent.
lines.en: All right. Let us warn the others, to keep them from worrying.

days.title: Nuancer en direct : les concessives orales
days.objective: You can nuance a spoken argument with certes... mais, avoir beau + infinitive, il n'empêche que, cela dit and n'en... pas moins.
days.paretoFocus: Live debate at B2 is won by conceding a point then holding your ground; these spoken concessive turns are distinct from the written opposition connectors already taught.
drills: Concede then object out loud twice using 'certes... mais'.
Say one 'il a beau + infinitive' sentence that concedes a quality but draws a contrary conclusion.
Reassert a point after a concession with 'il n'en reste pas moins que'.

items: What does 'avoir beau + infinitive' express?
Which spoken phrases let you concede a point before objecting?
How is 'cela dit' used to soften a disagreement?

activities.title: Concéder puis objecter
activities.intro: Spoken French nuances an argument with fixed concessive turns: certes... mais grants then counters, avoir beau + infinitive concedes a fact while announcing a contrary conclusion, il n'empêche que, together with n'en... pas moins, reasserts a point, and cela dit tempers what was just said.

items.hr: Certes, c'est cher, mais la qualité est là.
items.en: Admittedly it is expensive, but the quality is there.

items.hr: Il a beau être doué, il ne travaille pas assez.
items.en: However talented he is, he does not work enough.

items.hr: C'est risqué ; il n'empêche que ça vaut la peine d'essayer.
items.en: It is risky; still, it is worth trying.
items.note: il n'empêche que reasserts a point

items.hr: Le projet est ambitieux ; il n'en reste pas moins réalisable.
items.en: The project is ambitious; it is nonetheless feasible.

items.hr: Cela dit, je comprends tes réserves.
items.en: That said, I understand your reservations.
items.note: cela dit tempers the previous statement

activities.title: Les tours concessifs
activities.intro: Practise the spoken concessive turns and tell them from opposition connectors.

questions.prompt: Choose the concessive structure meaning 'however talented he is'.
questions.explanation: avoir beau + infinitive concedes a point while announcing a contrary conclusion.

questions.prompt: In careful writing, which connector marks OPPOSITION rather than consequence?
questions.explanation: néanmoins signals opposition; the other three announce a consequence.

questions.prompt: Concede then object with a fixed phrase. Complete: Il n'____ pas moins vrai que le budget est limité.
questions.explanation: il n'en est pas moins vrai que means 'it is nonetheless true that'.

questions.prompt: Reach back to the subjunctive after a concessive conjunction: Bien qu'il ____ raison, personne ne l'écoute. (avoir)
questions.explanation: bien que triggers the subjunctive: ait.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'It is expensive, admittedly, but it is worth it.'
questions.explanation: certes concedes the cost before mais introduces the counter-point.

activities.title: Débat sur le télétravail
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You debate remote work, conceding points then holding your position out loud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le télétravail, c'est l'avenir, non ?
lines.en: Remote work is the future, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certes, c'est pratique, mais il isole beaucoup de gens.
lines.en: Admittedly it is convenient, but it isolates a lot of people.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On a beau être seul, on gagne un temps fou sur les trajets.
lines.en: However alone you are, you save a huge amount of commuting time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est vrai, il n'empêche que le lien avec l'équipe se perd.
lines.en: True, and yet the bond with the team gets lost.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cela dit, on peut se voir une fois par semaine.
lines.en: That said, we can meet once a week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord avec toi. Ce n'en est pas moins un vrai changement de culture.
lines.en: I agree with you. It is nonetheless a real change of culture.

days.title: Débattre des chiffres : commenter des données
days.objective: You can describe and interpret figures and a chart, using proportions, trend verbs and phrases that attribute a cause to the data.
days.paretoFocus: B2 oral and written triggers are often a chart or a set of figures; you must read a trend, quote a proportion and explain a variation to argue from data.
drills: Describe a made-up trend out loud using 'augmenter de', 'chuter' and 'stagner'.
State one proportion three ways: with la majorité, un tiers and la moitié.
Attribute one trend to a cause using 's'expliquer par'.

items: Which verb describes a sharp fall, and which a steady, flat line?
How do you state a percentage increase in French?
How do you attribute a trend to its cause?

activities.title: Lire et commenter des données
activities.intro: To comment on figures, use proportion words (la majorité, un tiers, la moitié), trend verbs (augmenter, diminuer, chuter, stagner, doubler), amounts introduced by de (une hausse de vingt pour cent) and cause phrases (cette baisse s'explique par).

items.hr: Selon ce graphique, la consommation a augmenté de trente pour cent en dix ans.
items.en: According to this graph, consumption rose by thirty percent in ten years.

items.hr: La majorité des personnes interrogées se disent favorables au projet.
items.en: The majority of those surveyed say they favour the project.

items.hr: Les ventes ont chuté au premier trimestre, puis elles ont stagné.
items.en: Sales dropped in the first quarter, then they stagnated.
items.note: chuter = fall sharply, stagner = stay flat

items.hr: Ces données montrent une nette tendance à la hausse.
items.en: This data shows a clear upward trend.

items.hr: Cette baisse s'explique en grande partie par la crise.
items.en: This fall is largely explained by the crisis.

activities.title: Commenter un graphique
activities.intro: Practise the language of figures, proportions and trends.

questions.prompt: Choose the verb for a sharp fall in a graph.
questions.explanation: chuter means to drop sharply; grimper is to climb, stagner to stay flat, doubler to double.

questions.prompt: Complete: ____ des votants ont choisi la première option. (a third)
questions.explanation: un tiers is one third; un quart a quarter, la moitié a half.

questions.prompt: Express a percentage rise. Complete: Le chiffre d'affaires a augmenté ____ vingt pour cent. (preposition)
questions.explanation: augmenter de + amount states the size of the rise.

questions.prompt: Reach back to the formal cause markers: Cette hausse s'explique ____ la reprise économique. (cause preposition)
questions.explanation: s'expliquer par introduces the cause of a trend.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'According to this graph, sales have doubled.'
questions.explanation: selon ce graphique opens the comment; doubler describes the trend.

activities.title: Commenter une courbe
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You comment on an attendance chart in a meeting, reading the trend and its cause.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Regarde cette courbe : la fréquentation a explosé cette année.
lines.en: Look at this curve: attendance has soared this year.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En effet, elle a presque doublé par rapport à l'an dernier.
lines.en: Indeed, it has nearly doubled compared with last year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: À quoi attribues-tu cette hausse ?
lines.en: What do you attribute this rise to?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Elle s'explique surtout par notre nouvelle campagne en ligne.
lines.en: It is mainly explained by our new online campaign.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourtant, en fin d'année, la tendance s'inverse.
lines.en: Yet at the end of the year the trend reverses.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, la majorité des visiteurs partent après les fêtes ; il faudra corriger ça.
lines.en: Yes, the majority of visitors leave after the holidays; we will have to fix that.

days.title: Compréhension de l'oral : l'exposé long
days.objective: You can follow a five-minute spoken presentation heard twice, recognise its structure markers and take notes on the main ideas and figures.
days.paretoFocus: The first listening document at this level is a long presentation played twice; catching its plan and key figures rather than every word is the skill that scores.
drills: Listen to any short talk and note only its plan and any numbers, not full sentences.
List three phrases a speaker uses to announce a plan or a conclusion.
After a second listening, check your notes against the speaker's final summary.

items: What should you write down on the first listening, and what on the second?
Which phrases signal that a speaker is about to conclude?
Why note figures and structure words first?

activities.title: Suivre un exposé
activities.intro: A long presentation is played twice, so use the first listening for the plan and main ideas, the second for details and figures. Speakers signpost their structure with phrases like je traiterai trois aspects, premièrement, d'une part, and close with en définitive or pour conclure.

items.hr: Dans un exposé, l'orateur annonce souvent son plan : « Je traiterai successivement trois aspects. »
items.en: In a presentation, the speaker often announces the plan: 'I will deal with three aspects in turn.'

items.hr: Les mots comme « d'une part... d'autre part » signalent les parties.
items.en: Words like 'on one hand... on the other' signal the parts.

items.hr: À la première écoute, notez les idées principales, pas chaque mot.
items.en: On the first listening, note the main ideas, not every word.

items.hr: « En définitive » ou « pour conclure » annoncent la synthèse finale.
items.en: 'In short' or 'to conclude' announce the final synthesis.

items.hr: Une reformulation (« autrement dit ») répète l'idée : profitez-en pour vérifier vos notes.
items.en: A reformulation ('in other words') repeats the idea: use it to check your notes.

activities.title: Repérer le plan et les chiffres
activities.intro: Practise spotting structure, conclusions and figures in a spoken presentation.

questions.prompt: In a presentation, which phrase signals that the speaker is about to conclude?
questions.explanation: en définitive introduces the closing synthesis; the others open or extend a point.

questions.prompt: You hear: « Le nombre d'adhérents est passé de mille à mille cinq cents. » What happened?
questions.explanation: From 1000 to 1500 is a rise of fifty percent, that is, half again as many.

questions.prompt: A speaker announces the structure. Complete: Je vais aborder ____ points : l'origine, les effets et les solutions. (number word for 3)
questions.explanation: trois points matches the three items the speaker then lists.

questions.prompt: The speaker reformulates. Complete the marker meaning 'in other words': ____ dit, la mesure a échoué.
questions.explanation: autrement dit introduces a reformulation of the previous idea.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'First I will explain the causes.'
questions.explanation: d'abord announces the first part of the presentation.

activities.title: Simuler l'écoute d'un exposé
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A study partner delivers a presentation while you identify its structure and note the figures.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Écoute mon plan : je traiterai d'abord les causes, puis les conséquences.
lines.en: Listen to my plan: I will deal first with the causes, then the consequences.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Très clair. Tu annonces deux parties, je note « causes / conséquences ».
lines.en: Very clear. You announce two parts, I write down 'causes / consequences'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Premièrement, la hausse des prix a réduit le pouvoir d'achat.
lines.en: Firstly, the rise in prices reduced purchasing power.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai noté le chiffre : moins cinq pour cent, c'est bien ça ?
lines.en: I noted the figure: minus five percent, is that right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exact. En définitive, les ménages consomment moins.
lines.en: Exactly. In short, households consume less.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, ta conclusion reprend l'idée principale ; mes notes sont complètes.
lines.en: Perfect, your conclusion restates the main idea; my notes are complete.

days.title: Compréhension de l'oral : l'interview en une écoute
days.objective: You can extract who speaks, their position and key facts from an interview or news bulletin heard only once, by reading the questions first.
days.paretoFocus: The second listening document is played a single time, so previewing the questions and listening for targeted information is what saves the marks.
drills: Before listening to any interview, read the questions and underline the key word in each.
Practise catching a speaker's stance from one phrase, such as 'not opposed but cautious'.
Report one thing an interviewee said, shifting the tense for indirect speech.

items: How many times is the second listening document played?
What should you do before it starts?
How do you identify a speaker's position quickly?

activities.title: Écouter une seule fois
activities.intro: The second document, an interview or a news bulletin, is heard only once. Read the questions first so you know what to listen for, identify who is speaking and their stance, and if you miss one answer, move on to the next without panicking.

items.hr: Le deuxième document n'est diffusé qu'une seule fois : lisez les questions avant.
items.en: The second document is played only once: read the questions beforehand.

items.hr: Dans une interview, repérez qui parle et quelle est sa position.
items.en: In an interview, identify who is speaking and their position.

items.hr: Un bulletin d'informations enchaîne plusieurs sujets brefs : gardez le fil.
items.en: A news bulletin runs through several short items: keep track.

items.hr: Les mots interrogatifs de la question (« pourquoi », « combien ») vous disent quoi écouter.
items.en: The question words ('why', 'how many') tell you what to listen for.

items.hr: Si vous manquez une réponse, passez à la suivante sans paniquer.
items.en: If you miss an answer, move to the next one without panicking.

activities.title: Cibler l'information
activities.intro: Practise targeted listening and reporting for a single-play document.

questions.prompt: The second listening document at this level is played:
questions.explanation: The second recording, an interview or news bulletin, is heard only once.

questions.prompt: A guest says: « Je ne suis pas opposé au projet, mais je reste prudent. » His position is:
questions.explanation: 'Not opposed but cautious' signals reserved, conditional support.

questions.prompt: Prepare before a single listening. Complete: Avant l'écoute, ____ les questions pour savoir quoi repérer. (imperative of lire, vous form)
questions.explanation: lisez les questions means 'read the questions' first.

questions.prompt: Reach back to reported speech and the tense shift: Il a déclaré qu'il ne ____ pas opposé au projet. (être, imperfect)
questions.explanation: In reported speech in the past, the present est shifts to the imperfect était.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Read the questions before listening.'
questions.explanation: The strategy is to preview the questions before the single listening.

activities.title: Simuler une interview en une écoute
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A partner plays an interview once while you catch the guest's stance and a date.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On n'a droit qu'à une seule écoute pour l'interview, prêt ?
lines.en: We only get one listening for the interview, ready?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'ai déjà lu les questions : on cherche son avis et une date.
lines.en: Yes, I have already read the questions: we are looking for her opinion and a date.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: « L'invitée pense que la réforme est utile mais mal préparée. » Tu retiens quoi ?
lines.en: 'The guest thinks the reform is useful but poorly prepared.' What do you take from that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Son avis est nuancé : elle approuve le but, pas la méthode.
lines.en: Her opinion is nuanced: she approves of the aim, not the method.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la date qu'elle cite pour l'application ?
lines.en: And the date she gives for it to take effect?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'an prochain, en septembre. Une seule écoute a suffi cette fois.
lines.en: Next year, in September. One listening was enough this time.

days.title: Compréhension des écrits : le texte informatif
days.objective: You can grasp the main idea and locate facts in an informative text on France or the French-speaking world, and guess unknown words from context.
days.paretoFocus: The first reading text is informative; finding the theme fast, locating specific facts and separating what the author states from what is reported are the marks that count.
drills: Read the title and first paragraph of any article and state its theme in one sentence.
Guess two unknown words from context and from words in the same family.
Separate one stated fact from one reported claim ('selon une étude') in a text.

items: Where is the theme of an informative text usually announced?
How can you guess an unknown word without a dictionary?
How do you tell what the author asserts from what they merely report?

activities.title: Lire pour s'informer
activities.intro: An informative text presents facts. Find the theme in the title and opening paragraph, follow the logical connectors, and work out unfamiliar vocabulary from the surrounding text and word families. Distinguish what the author asserts from what they attribute to a source (selon une étude).

items.hr: Un texte informatif présente des faits : cherchez le thème dès le titre.
items.en: An informative text presents facts: look for the theme from the title.

items.hr: La francophonie désigne l'ensemble des pays et des personnes qui parlent français.
items.en: The French-speaking world means all the countries and people who speak French.

items.hr: Repérez les connecteurs logiques pour suivre le raisonnement.
items.en: Spot the logical connectors to follow the reasoning.

items.hr: Un mot inconnu se devine grâce au contexte et aux mots de la même famille.
items.en: An unknown word can be guessed from context and words in the same family.

items.hr: Distinguez ce que l'auteur affirme de ce qu'il rapporte (« selon une étude »).
items.en: Tell what the author asserts from what they report ('according to a study').

activities.title: Comprendre l'information
activities.intro: Practise reading strategies and topic vocabulary for an informative text.

questions.prompt: In an informative text, the fastest way to grasp the main idea is to:
options: read the title and first paragraph
questions.answer: read the title and first paragraph
questions.explanation: The theme is usually announced in the title and opening paragraph.

questions.prompt: The text reads: « Le français est une langue en plein essor en Afrique. » 'En plein essor' means:
questions.explanation: en plein essor means booming or expanding strongly.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun for someone who speaks a language: Le français compte plus de trois cents millions de ____.
questions.explanation: un locuteur is a speaker; here the plural locuteurs.

questions.prompt: Reach back to nominalisation. Turn the verb into a noun: Le français se développe donne le ____ du français. (from développer)
questions.explanation: The nominalisation of se développer is le développement.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'French is a growing language in Africa.'
questions.explanation: en expansion describes the growth of the language.

activities.title: Un article sur la francophonie
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You discuss an informative article on the French language worldwide.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai lu un article sur la place du français dans le monde.
lines.en: I read an article on the place of French in the world.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ah, la francophonie m'intéresse. Quelle était l'idée principale ?
lines.en: Oh, the French-speaking world interests me. What was the main idea?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que le nombre de locuteurs augmente surtout en Afrique.
lines.en: That the number of speakers is rising, especially in Africa.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est un texte informatif ou une tribune d'opinion ?
lines.en: Is it an informative text or an opinion piece?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Informatif : l'auteur cite des chiffres, sans donner son avis.
lines.en: Informative: the author quotes figures, without giving an opinion.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait pour s'entraîner à repérer les faits et les sources.
lines.en: Perfect for practising how to spot facts and sources.

days.title: Compréhension des écrits : le texte argumentatif
days.objective: You can identify an author's thesis, spot concessions and counter-arguments, and detect irony in an argumentative or polemical text.
days.paretoFocus: The second reading text argues a case; finding the thesis, following the concessions and catching irony are exactly what the questions test.
drills: Read any opinion piece and state the author's thesis in one sentence.
Underline one concession ('certes... mais') and the counter-argument that follows it.
Find one ironic sentence and say what the author really means.

items: What is the thesis of an argumentative text?
What does a concession do before the author objects?
How do you detect irony so you do not misread the author's view?

activities.title: Lire un texte qui argumente
activities.intro: An argumentative text defends a thesis. The author often concedes the opposing view (certes... mais) before refuting it, marks turns with cependant, en revanche or or, and may use irony, saying the opposite of what is meant. The conclusion usually reveals the final stance.

items.hr: Dans un texte argumentatif, l'auteur défend une thèse.
items.en: In an argumentative text, the author defends a thesis.

items.hr: Une concession (« certes... mais ») reconnaît l'avis adverse avant de le réfuter.
items.en: A concession ('admittedly... but') acknowledges the opposing view before refuting it.

items.hr: Les connecteurs « cependant », « en revanche », « or » marquent les tournants du raisonnement.
items.en: The connectors 'however', 'on the other hand', 'yet' mark the turns of the reasoning.

items.hr: L'ironie dit le contraire de ce qu'on pense : repérez le ton.
items.en: Irony says the opposite of what is meant: watch the tone.

items.hr: La conclusion révèle souvent la position finale de l'auteur, cherchez-la.
items.en: The conclusion often reveals the author's final position, look for it.

activities.title: Repérer la thèse et les concessions
activities.intro: Practise finding the thesis, concessions and irony in an argumentative text.

questions.prompt: The author's thesis in an argumentative text is:
questions.explanation: The thesis is the stance the author argues for throughout.

questions.prompt: Which sentence CONCEDES a point before objecting?
options: « C'est faux du début à la fin. »
« Certes, l'idée est séduisante, mais elle coûte cher. »
questions.answer: « Certes, l'idée est séduisante, mais elle coûte cher. »
questions.explanation: certes... mais grants the opposing view, then counters it.

questions.prompt: Name the author's defended opinion in one word: L'auteur défend une ____ selon laquelle la ville doit se transformer.
questions.explanation: la thèse is the position the author argues for.

questions.prompt: Reach back to the relative pronoun after a preposition: L'idée pour ____ il se bat est ambitieuse.
questions.explanation: After pour, referring to une idée, the relative is laquelle.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The author defends a debatable thesis.'
questions.explanation: défendre une thèse is the core action of an argumentative text.

activities.title: Décortiquer une tribune
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You dissect a polemical op-ed with a study partner, naming the thesis and the irony.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ce texte est clairement à charge contre les voitures en ville.
lines.en: This text is clearly an attack on cars in the city.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, la thèse est nette : il faut les interdire au centre.
lines.en: Yes, the thesis is clear: they should be banned from the centre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourtant l'auteur concède que les commerçants vont souffrir.
lines.en: Yet the author concedes that shopkeepers will suffer.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Justement, « certes... mais » : il reconnaît le problème avant de le balayer.
lines.en: Exactly, 'admittedly... but': he acknowledges the problem before brushing it aside.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et cette phrase sur « nos chers automobilistes », c'est sérieux ?
lines.en: And that phrase about 'our dear motorists', is it serious?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, c'est ironique : il pense exactement le contraire.
lines.en: No, it is ironic: he means exactly the opposite.

days.title: Production écrite : la contribution à un débat
days.objective: You can write a 250-word contribution to an online debate, stating a clear position, backing it with examples and conceding a point to sound nuanced.
days.paretoFocus: One of the three written task types is a reasoned contribution to a debate; a clear stance, supported arguments and a concession are what a strong text needs.
drills: Draft an opening sentence stating your position on a topic with 'à mon sens'.
Support one argument with a concrete example introduced by 'par exemple'.
Write one concession with 'il est vrai que... néanmoins' before restating your view.

items: How should a debate contribution open?
Why include a concession to the opposing view?
What is the minimum length for this written task?

activities.title: Contribuer à un débat
activities.intro: A debate contribution opens with a clear personal stance, supports each argument with a concrete example, and concedes an opposing point to appear nuanced before restating the position. The register is polite and semi-formal, and the text runs at least 250 words.

items.hr: Une contribution à un débat commence par une prise de position claire.
items.en: A debate contribution begins with a clear stance.

items.hr: « À mon sens » ou « selon moi » introduisent votre opinion sans agressivité.
items.en: 'To my mind' or 'in my opinion' introduce your view without aggression.

items.hr: Appuyez chaque argument d'un exemple concret : « par exemple », « ainsi ».
items.en: Back each argument with a concrete example: 'for example', 'thus'.

items.hr: Concédez un point adverse : « il est vrai que... néanmoins ».
items.en: Concede an opposing point: 'it is true that... nonetheless'.

items.hr: Une contribution réussie fait au moins deux cent cinquante mots et reste polie.
items.en: A good contribution runs at least two hundred and fifty words and stays polite.

activities.title: Structurer sa contribution
activities.intro: Practise the moves of a written debate contribution.

questions.prompt: A good online debate contribution opens with:
questions.explanation: State your position first, then support it.

questions.prompt: Which phrase best introduces your opinion in a semi-formal forum?
questions.explanation: à mon sens is courteous and appropriate; the others are rude or unfounded.

questions.prompt: State your stance politely. Complete: ____ moi, cette mesure est nécessaire. (preposition introducing an opinion)
questions.explanation: selon moi means 'in my opinion'.

questions.prompt: Add an illustration. Complete: Beaucoup de villes l'ont fait ; ____ exemple, plusieurs capitales européennes.
questions.explanation: par exemple introduces an example.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'In my opinion, this measure is useful.'
questions.explanation: à mon sens is a polite way to open with your opinion.

activities.title: Préparer une contribution
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You plan a debate contribution with a partner, choosing a stance, an example and a concession.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le sujet du débat, c'est l'interdiction des trottinettes en ville.
lines.en: The debate topic is banning scooters in the city.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vais prendre position contre l'interdiction, mais avec nuance.
lines.en: I will take a stand against the ban, but with nuance.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as des arguments concrets ?
lines.en: Do you have concrete arguments?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui : par exemple, elles remplacent des trajets en voiture.
lines.en: Yes: for example, they replace car trips.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et tu concèdes quelque chose à l'adversaire ?
lines.en: And do you concede anything to the other side?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr : il est vrai qu'elles gênent les piétons, néanmoins on peut mieux les encadrer.
lines.en: Of course: it is true they get in pedestrians' way, nonetheless they can be better regulated.

days.title: Production écrite : la lettre formelle
days.objective: You can write a formal letter to an authority, using the fixed salutation, an object line, a polite request and the codified closing formula.
days.paretoFocus: Writing to an administration is one of the three written task types; its ritual salutation and closing are graded, and getting them wrong signals a register error.
drills: Write the opening salutation for a recipient whose name you do not know.
Draft an object line for a request to a mayor or a director.
Write out the fixed closing formula from memory.

items: Which salutation fits an unknown official recipient?
What is the standard closing formula of a formal letter?
Which register and pronoun run through the whole letter?

activities.title: Les rituels de la lettre formelle
activities.intro: A formal letter opens with Madame, Monsieur when the recipient is unknown, states its object in one line, sets out the request politely (je me permets de vous solliciter), and closes with the fixed formula je vous prie d'agréer, Madame, Monsieur, mes salutations distinguées. The whole body uses vous and an elevated register.

items.hr: Une lettre formelle s'ouvre par « Madame, Monsieur, » si l'on ignore le destinataire.
items.en: A formal letter opens with 'Madam, Sir,' if the recipient is unknown.

items.hr: Indiquez l'objet en une ligne : « Objet : demande d'aménagement d'un passage piéton. »
items.en: State the object in one line: 'Subject: request to install a pedestrian crossing.'

items.hr: Exposez la demande poliment : « Je me permets de vous solliciter au sujet de... »
items.en: Set out the request politely: 'I take the liberty of contacting you about...'

items.hr: La formule de congé est figée : « Je vous prie d'agréer, Madame, Monsieur, mes salutations distinguées. »
items.en: The closing formula is fixed: 'Please accept, Madam, Sir, my best regards.'

items.hr: Tout le corps est au vouvoiement et dans un registre soutenu.
items.en: The whole body uses vous and an elevated register.

activities.title: Les codes de la lettre
activities.intro: Practise the salutation, request and closing of a formal letter.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct formal closing for a letter to an official.
options: « Je vous prie d'agréer, Madame, Monsieur, mes salutations distinguées. »
questions.answer: « Je vous prie d'agréer, Madame, Monsieur, mes salutations distinguées. »
questions.explanation: This fixed formula is the standard courteous closing of a formal letter.

questions.prompt: Which opening salutation suits a recipient whose name you do not know?
questions.explanation: Madame, Monsieur addresses an unknown official politely.

questions.prompt: Introduce a polite request. Complete with the infinitive meaning 'to request formally': Je me permets de vous ____ au sujet du stationnement.
questions.explanation: solliciter means to make a formal request.

questions.prompt: Reach back to the conditional of politeness. Complete: Je vous ____ reconnaissant de bien vouloir intervenir. (être, conditional, first person)
questions.explanation: je vous serais reconnaissant, 'I would be grateful', softens the request.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I am writing to you about the school canteen.'
questions.explanation: au sujet de introduces the reason for the letter.

activities.title: Écrire au maire
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You explain to a friend how you are drafting a formal letter to the mayor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu écris vraiment au maire pour le passage piéton ?
lines.en: Are you really writing to the mayor about the pedestrian crossing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, en lettre formelle. Je commence par « Madame, Monsieur ».
lines.en: Yes, as a formal letter. I begin with 'Madam, Sir'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et tu expliques quoi, exactement ?
lines.en: And what exactly do you explain?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je me permets de le solliciter pour sécuriser la rue de l'école.
lines.en: I take the liberty of asking him to make the school street safe.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: N'oublie pas la formule de politesse à la fin.
lines.en: Don't forget the closing formula at the end.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr : « Je vous prie d'agréer mes salutations distinguées. »
lines.en: Of course: 'Please accept my best regards.'

days.title: Production écrite : l'article critique
days.objective: You can write a 250-word critical article that briefly presents its subject, then delivers a balanced, criteria-based judgment and a recommendation.
days.paretoFocus: The critical article is the third written task type; it must argue an evaluation with precise criteria, not just assert a taste, and it must weigh strengths against weaknesses.
drills: Write an opening line that briefly presents a film, book or event you could review.
Give one strength and one weakness of it, each justified by a criterion.
Write a nuanced closing recommendation ('à voir, malgré ses défauts').

items: What should a critical article base its judgment on?
Why weigh strengths against weaknesses?
How does a critique differ from simply saying 'I liked it'?

activities.title: Porter un jugement argumenté
activities.intro: A critical article first presents its subject briefly, then delivers a reasoned judgment using evaluative vocabulary (réussi, décevant, convaincant, prévisible). It balances strengths and weaknesses, justifies the verdict with precise criteria, and ends on a nuanced recommendation.

items.hr: Un article critique présente d'abord brièvement l'objet, puis porte un jugement argumenté.
items.en: A critical article first briefly presents the subject, then gives a reasoned judgment.

items.hr: Employez un vocabulaire d'évaluation : « réussi », « décevant », « convaincant », « prévisible ».
items.en: Use evaluative vocabulary: 'well made', 'disappointing', 'convincing', 'predictable'.

items.hr: Équilibrez le propos : soulignez les points forts et les points faibles.
items.en: Balance the piece: highlight the strengths and the weaknesses.

items.hr: Justifiez votre avis par des critères précis, pas par un simple « j'aime ».
items.en: Justify your view with precise criteria, not just 'I like it'.

items.hr: Terminez par une recommandation nuancée : « à voir, malgré ses défauts ».
items.en: End on a nuanced recommendation: 'worth seeing, despite its flaws'.

activities.title: Évaluer avec des critères
activities.intro: Practise the evaluative moves and vocabulary of a critical article.

questions.prompt: A critical article should base its judgment on:
questions.explanation: A critique argues its evaluation; it does not merely assert a preference.

questions.prompt: Which adjective expresses a negative judgment?
questions.explanation: décevant means disappointing; the others are positive.

questions.prompt: Give a verdict with an evaluative adjective meaning 'disappointing', agreeing with 'la fin' (feminine): La fin du film est ____.
questions.explanation: Agreeing with fin, feminine singular, the adjective becomes décevante.

questions.prompt: Reach back to mise en relief. Emphasise a strong point: Ce qui frappe le plus, ____ la qualité des images.
questions.explanation: The structure ce qui... c'est... highlights the key element.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'This film is well made but predictable.'
questions.explanation: réussi and prévisible weigh a strength against a weakness.

activities.title: Écrire une critique
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You discuss writing a balanced review of an exhibition.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as vu la nouvelle exposition ? Tu en penses quoi ?
lines.en: Have you seen the new exhibition? What do you think of it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vais en faire une critique : réussie sur la forme, décevante sur le fond.
lines.en: I am going to review it: well done in form, disappointing in substance.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu vas quand même reconnaître ses qualités ?
lines.en: Will you still acknowledge its strengths?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, un article critique doit être équilibré, pas un simple coup de gueule.
lines.en: Yes, a critical article must be balanced, not just a rant.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quel est son plus gros défaut, selon toi ?
lines.en: What is its biggest flaw, in your view?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ce qui manque, c'est un vrai fil conducteur entre les salles.
lines.en: What is missing is a real thread linking the rooms.

days.title: Production écrite : relecture et autocorrection
days.objective: You can self-correct a written text with a checklist covering agreement, accents, word count and repetition, in two focused rereadings.
days.paretoFocus: Self-correction is a named descriptor at this level; catching your own agreement, accent and length errors before handing in recovers marks no examiner will give back.
drills: Reread one of your texts once for meaning, then once for language only.
Scan a text for the accent traps a/à and ou/où and fix any wrong one.
Count the words of a text and check it clears the required minimum.

items: In what order should you reread: language first or meaning first?
Which accent pairs change the meaning of a word?
What is the penalty for falling short of the word minimum?

activities.title: Se relire efficacement
activities.intro: Reread once for meaning and clarity, then again for language: check subject-verb and past-participle agreement, hunt accent traps (a and à, ou and où), count the words against the minimum, and replace repetitions with synonyms or pronouns.

items.hr: Relisez toujours votre texte une fois pour le sens, une fois pour la langue.
items.en: Always reread your text once for meaning, once for language.

items.hr: Vérifiez les accords : sujet-verbe, et accord du participe passé.
items.en: Check the agreements: subject-verb, and past-participle agreement.

items.hr: Traquez les accents : « a » et « à », « ou » et « où » changent le sens.
items.en: Hunt the accents: 'a' and 'à', 'ou' and 'où' change the meaning.

items.hr: Comptez vos mots : en dessous du minimum, le texte est pénalisé.
items.en: Count your words: below the minimum, the text is penalised.

items.hr: Remplacez les répétitions par des synonymes ou des pronoms.
items.en: Replace repetitions with synonyms or pronouns.

activities.title: La liste de contrôle
activities.intro: Practise the self-correction checklist on real traps.

questions.prompt: During self-correction, a smart order is to check:
questions.explanation: Read once for content and clarity, then again for grammar and spelling.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correctly accented?
options: « Il va a Paris ou il travaille. »
« Il va à Paris où il travaille. »
« Il va à Paris ou il travaille. »
« Il va a Paris où il travaille. »
questions.answer: « Il va à Paris où il travaille. »
questions.explanation: à (preposition) and où (relative 'where') both need their accents here.

questions.prompt: Fix the accent: choose the right word for 'where'. Complete: La ville ____ je suis né est petite.
questions.explanation: où with the accent means 'where'; ou without means 'or'.

questions.prompt: Check the past-participle agreement with a preposed feminine plural object: Les lettres que j'ai ____ (écrire) sont parties.
questions.explanation: With avoir the participle agrees with the preposed object que, les lettres: écrites.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I reread my text before handing it in.'
questions.explanation: avant de + infinitive places the rereading before handing in.

activities.title: Atelier de relecture
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You help a partner self-correct a text, focusing on accents and word count.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai fini mon texte, mais je ne suis pas sûr des accents.
lines.en: I have finished my text, but I am not sure about the accents.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Relis-le deux fois : d'abord le sens, ensuite la langue.
lines.en: Reread it twice: first the meaning, then the language.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tiens, ici j'ai écrit « a » sans accent devant un lieu.
lines.en: Look, here I wrote 'a' without an accent before a place.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Corrige : « à Paris » prend un accent, sinon c'est le verbe avoir.
lines.en: Correct it: 'à Paris' takes an accent, otherwise it is the verb 'to have'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et tu comptes tes mots, toi ?
lines.en: And do you count your words?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Toujours : en dessous de deux cent cinquante, on perd des points.
lines.en: Always: below two hundred and fifty, you lose points.

days.title: Production orale : construire son plan
days.objective: You can turn a short trigger document into a defended outline in the thirty-minute preparation, with a problematic question, two or three arguments and a conclusion.
days.paretoFocus: The oral gives thirty minutes to prepare from a short document; framing the issue and building a keyword outline is what lets you speak naturally rather than read.
drills: Read any short opinion snippet and write the problematic question it raises.
Choose a position and jot two or three arguments, each with an example, as keywords only.
Sketch an intro, a structured development and an open conclusion in note form.

items: What is your first task with a trigger document?
Why write keywords instead of full sentences for the oral?
What three parts should your outline contain?

activities.title: Préparer un exposé en trente minutes
activities.intro: You have thirty minutes to prepare a talk from a short document. First identify the issue it raises and phrase it as a clear question (la problématique), then pick a position and back it with two or three examples. Note only keywords, and plan an introduction, a structured development and an open conclusion.

items.hr: Vous disposez de trente minutes pour préparer votre exposé à partir d'un court document.
items.en: You have thirty minutes to prepare your talk from a short document.

items.hr: Dégagez d'abord le problème posé : formulez une question claire.
items.en: First identify the problem raised: phrase a clear question.

items.hr: Choisissez une position et deux ou trois arguments, chacun avec un exemple.
items.en: Choose a position and two or three arguments, each with an example.

items.hr: Notez seulement des mots-clés, pas des phrases entières, pour rester naturel.
items.en: Note only keywords, not full sentences, to stay natural.

items.hr: Prévoyez une introduction, un développement structuré et une conclusion ouverte.
items.en: Plan an introduction, a structured development and an open conclusion.

activities.title: De la préparation au plan
activities.intro: Practise building a structured, defensible plan from a single source text.

questions.prompt: With a trigger document, your first task is to:
questions.explanation: The talk must respond to the issue the document raises.

questions.prompt: For the oral, your notes should be:
questions.explanation: Keywords keep the delivery natural; reading full sentences sounds wooden.

questions.prompt: Name the central question you must formulate. Complete: Il faut dégager la ____ posée par le document. (French word for the core issue)
questions.explanation: la problématique is the key question your talk answers.

questions.prompt: Announce your first argument with a linking word meaning 'firstly': ____, la mesure crée des emplois.
questions.explanation: premièrement introduces the first argument of the development.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'First I will present the advantages.'
questions.explanation: d'abord opens the structured development of the talk.

activities.title: Préparer à partir d'un document
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A partner checks how you turn a trigger document into an outline.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ton document parle du gaspillage alimentaire. Par quoi tu commences ?
lines.en: Your document is about food waste. Where do you begin?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je dégage la problématique : comment réduire ce gaspillage au quotidien ?
lines.en: I frame the issue: how can we cut this waste day to day?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ta position ?
lines.en: And your position?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je défends l'idée que les petits gestes suffisent, avec deux arguments.
lines.en: I defend the idea that small actions are enough, with two arguments.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu écris tout ton texte pour la préparation ?
lines.en: Do you write out your whole text for the preparation?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, juste des mots-clés, sinon je lirais au lieu de parler.
lines.en: No, just keywords, otherwise I would read instead of speaking.

days.title: Production orale : défendre son point de vue
days.objective: You can defend your presentation under the examiners' questions, reformulating, conceding valid points and holding your position courteously.
days.paretoFocus: After the talk comes a debate with the examiners; a confident, nuanced defence that reformulates questions and concedes fair points scores far better than either surrender or aggression.
drills: Reformulate a challenging question out loud with 'Si je comprends bien, vous demandez si...'.
Concede one fair objection, then reassert your position with 'il n'en reste pas moins que'.
Buy thinking time politely with 'C'est une bonne question, laissez-moi y réfléchir'.

items: How should you react when an examiner challenges your view?
What does reformulating a question achieve?
How do you concede a point without abandoning your position?

activities.title: Défendre et débattre
activities.intro: After the presentation the examiner asks questions: listen to the end, reformulate to check understanding and buy time, defend your view with assurance while conceding a valid point, and conclude by restating your position without aggression.

items.hr: Après l'exposé, l'examinateur vous pose des questions : écoutez-les jusqu'au bout.
items.en: After the talk, the examiner asks questions: listen to them fully.

items.hr: Reformulez la question si besoin : « Si je comprends bien, vous me demandez si... »
items.en: Reformulate the question if needed: 'If I understand correctly, you are asking whether...'
items.note: reformulate to check and gain time

items.hr: Défendez votre avis avec assurance, mais reconnaissez un bon argument adverse.
items.en: Defend your view with assurance, but acknowledge a good opposing argument.

items.hr: Gagnez du temps poliment : « C'est une bonne question, laissez-moi y réfléchir. »
items.en: Buy time politely: 'That is a good question, let me think about it.'

items.hr: Concluez en réaffirmant votre position, sans agressivité.
items.en: Conclude by reasserting your position, without aggression.

activities.title: Tenir sa position
activities.intro: Practise defending a view under questioning.

questions.prompt: When an examiner challenges your view, the best reaction is to:
options: defend it while conceding valid points
questions.answer: defend it while conceding valid points
questions.explanation: A confident but nuanced defence scores best.

questions.prompt: Which phrase reformulates a question to check understanding?
options: « Si je comprends bien, vous demandez si... »
questions.answer: « Si je comprends bien, vous demandez si... »
questions.explanation: Reformulating shows you understood and buys thinking time.

questions.prompt: Buy time politely. Complete with the imperative of laisser (vous form): C'est une bonne question, ____-moi y réfléchir un instant.
questions.explanation: laissez-moi réfléchir gives you a courteous pause.

questions.prompt: Reach back to the concessive turn. Concede then hold your ground: Vous avez raison sur ce point ; il n'____ pas moins que ma conclusion tient.
questions.explanation: il n'en reste pas moins que reasserts your point after a concession.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I understand your objection, but I disagree.'
questions.explanation: Acknowledge the objection, then state your disagreement.

activities.title: Face aux questions du jury
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An examiner challenges your presentation and you defend it, conceding and holding firm.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous dites que les petits gestes suffisent. N'est-ce pas un peu naïf ?
lines.en: You say small actions are enough. Isn't that a bit naive?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si je comprends bien, vous doutez de leur impact réel ?
lines.en: If I understand correctly, you doubt their real impact?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exactement. Sans lois contraignantes, rien ne change.
lines.en: Exactly. Without binding laws, nothing changes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vous avez raison sur les limites ; il n'en reste pas moins que l'exemple individuel entraîne les autres.
lines.en: You are right about the limits; it is nonetheless true that individual example pulls others along.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Admettons. Mais comment mesurer cet effet ?
lines.en: Granted. But how do you measure that effect?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est une bonne question, laissez-moi préciser mon idée avec un exemple.
lines.en: That is a good question, let me clarify my point with an example.

days.title: Examen blanc : niveau B2
days.objective: You can run through the four parts of the level-B2 exam under real conditions, and you know the format, the pass rule and the word and time requirements.
days.paretoFocus: This exam is the level that now decides naturalisation, so knowing that you need half the total with no eliminatory score, a 250-word written text and a thirty-minute oral preparation is what keeps you in control on the day.
drills: Time yourself on one written task and confirm it clears the 250-word minimum.
Recite the pass rule: the overall minimum and the eliminatory score per part.
Plan how you would split the collective papers across the allotted time.

items: How many parts does the exam have and how is each scored?
What is the pass rule, including the eliminatory threshold?
Which written and oral requirements must you respect?

activities.title: Connaître l'épreuve
activities.intro: The exam has four parts, each marked out of twenty-five. You pass with at least fifty out of a hundred overall and never below five out of twenty-five in any part. The written production is at least two hundred and fifty words, the oral is prepared in thirty minutes, and the collective papers last two and a half hours.

items.hr: L'examen comporte quatre épreuves, chacune notée sur vingt-cinq.
items.en: The exam has four parts, each marked out of twenty-five.

items.hr: Il faut au moins cinquante points sur cent, et jamais moins de cinq sur vingt-cinq à une épreuve.
items.en: You need at least fifty out of a hundred, and never below five out of twenty-five in a part.

items.hr: À l'écrit, la production fait au minimum deux cent cinquante mots.
items.en: In writing, the production is at least two hundred and fifty words.

items.hr: À l'oral, vous préparez votre exposé pendant trente minutes.
items.en: For the oral, you prepare your talk for thirty minutes.

items.hr: Gérez votre temps : les épreuves collectives durent deux heures trente au total.
items.en: Manage your time: the collective papers last two and a half hours in total.

activities.title: Format et règles
activities.intro: Check the format, pass rule and requirements of the exam.

questions.prompt: To pass, a candidate needs:
options: 50/100 overall and at least 5/25 in each part
questions.answer: 50/100 overall and at least 5/25 in each part
questions.explanation: You need half the total and no eliminatory score, below 5/25, in any of the four parts.

questions.prompt: Which task is NOT part of this level-B2 written exam?
questions.explanation: Combining two documents into a synthesis is a higher-level task; here the written production is a single argued text.

questions.prompt: State the written minimum. Complete: La production écrite doit compter au moins deux cent ____ mots.
questions.explanation: The minimum is two hundred and fifty words.

questions.prompt: Reach back to the past infinitive. Complete with the auxiliary of avoir: Après ____ terminé l'écrit, on passe à l'oral.
questions.explanation: The past infinitive après avoir terminé places one part before the next.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'You must get at least half of the points.'
questions.explanation: The pass rule requires at least half the total points.

activities.title: La veille de l'examen
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. The day before the mock, you and a partner check the format and correct one misconception.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Demain, examen blanc complet. Tu connais bien le format ?
lines.en: Tomorrow, full mock exam. Do you know the format well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui : quatre épreuves sur vingt-cinq, et cinquante sur cent pour réussir.
lines.en: Yes: four parts out of twenty-five, and fifty out of a hundred to pass.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Attention, en dessous de cinq sur vingt-cinq à une épreuve, c'est éliminatoire.
lines.en: Careful, below five out of twenty-five in a part is eliminatory.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je sais. Et à l'écrit, je vise bien deux cent cinquante mots minimum.
lines.en: I know. And in writing, I am aiming for at least two hundred and fifty words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On nous demande une synthèse de deux textes, non ?
lines.en: We are asked for a synthesis of two texts, right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, à ce niveau c'est un seul texte argumenté ; la synthèse, c'est pour plus tard.
lines.en: No, at this level it is a single argued text; the synthesis comes later.

days.title: Le changement climatique et la responsabilité
days.objective: Argue whether responsibility for the climate lies with individuals or with institutions, conceding and rebutting.
days.paretoFocus: Climate is the debate topic of the decade; being able to defend a nuanced position on who must act is essential for the exam and for civic life.
drills: Write one sentence claiming individual action matters, then one anticipating the objection that big polluters matter more.
Use en raison de and quand bien même each in a full sentence about the environment.
Put today's three new words (empreinte, contraignant, insoutenable) into sentences you make up yourself.

items: Which connector introduces a concession you will then rebut with mais?
How do you say carbon footprint in French?
Give a soutenu way to say owing to the urgency.

activities.title: Agir pour le climat
activities.intro: Each sentence states a position, gives a reason, and anticipates an objection. Notice the soutenu connectors and the impersonal constructions that raise the register above everyday speech.

items.hr: Réduire son empreinte carbone est un devoir, non parce que chacun changera le climat à lui seul, mais parce qu'un comportement responsable finit par entraîner celui des autres.
items.en: Reducing one's carbon footprint is a duty, not because anyone will change the climate alone, but because responsible behaviour ends up prompting that of others.

items.hr: Certes, les gestes du quotidien pèsent peu face aux grands pollueurs, mais les négliger reviendrait à dédouaner tout le monde de ses obligations.
items.en: Admittedly, everyday actions weigh little against big polluters, but neglecting them would amount to letting everyone off the hook.

items.hr: En raison de l'urgence, beaucoup réclament des mesures contraignantes, quand bien même celles-ci limiteraient certaines libertés.
items.en: Owing to the urgency, many are calling for binding measures, even though these would restrict certain freedoms.
items.note: quand bien même + conditional = even if

items.hr: Il serait naïf de croire que la technologie résoudra seule la crise, car aucune innovation ne compense un mode de vie insoutenable.
items.en: It would be naive to believe technology alone will solve the crisis, for no innovation makes up for an unsustainable way of life.

items.hr: On oppose souvent l'écologie à l'économie, alors que la transition pourrait, à terme, créer plus d'emplois qu'elle n'en détruit.
items.en: People often set ecology against the economy, whereas the transition could, in the long run, create more jobs than it destroys.
items.note: à terme = in the long run; ne explétif after plus que

activities.title: Défendre une position sur le climat
activities.intro: Drill climate vocabulary while linking ideas with concession, cause and hypothesis.

questions.prompt: Face à l'urgence écologique, il faut adopter un mode de vie plus ___ si l'on veut préserver les ressources.
questions.explanation: Un mode de vie durable is sustainable; the other options describe waste, not sustainability.

questions.prompt: Rappel du subjonctif : il est indispensable que chacun ___ sa part d'effort.
questions.explanation: Il est indispensable que expresses necessity and triggers the subjunctive: que chacun prenne.

questions.prompt: Chaque citoyen devrait diminuer son ___ carbone pour limiter les rejets de gaz.
questions.explanation: Son empreinte carbone is the measure of one's impact on the climate.

questions.prompt: Rappel de l'hypothèse : si nous avions agi plus tôt, nous ___ (éviter) une partie des dégâts.
questions.explanation: Si + plus-que-parfait pairs with the conditionnel passé: nous aurions évité.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Cutting our emissions is a shared responsibility.'
questions.explanation: The infinitive Réduire works as the subject, followed by est une responsabilité partagée.

activities.title: Trier ou pas ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner doubts that individual effort matters; concede the point, then rebut it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Franchement, trier ses déchets ne changera rien tant que les industries polluent autant.
lines.en: Honestly, sorting your rubbish will change nothing as long as industry pollutes so much.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je comprends ce scepticisme, mais si chacun raisonnait ainsi, plus personne ne bougerait.
lines.en: I understand that scepticism, but if everyone thought that way, nobody would move at all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: N'empêche que les vrais responsables, ce sont les grands groupes, pas les particuliers.
lines.en: Still, the ones really responsible are the big corporations, not private individuals.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Certes, leur part est écrasante, or les politiques suivent souvent la pression des citoyens.
lines.en: Admittedly, their share is overwhelming, yet policy often follows pressure from citizens.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu crois vraiment qu'un consommateur pèse face à un tel secteur industriel ?
lines.en: Do you really think one consumer counts against such an industrial sector?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pris isolément, non ; mais en raison du nombre, nos choix finissent par compter.
lines.en: Taken alone, no; but because of sheer numbers, our choices end up counting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Admettons... à condition que l'État impose enfin des règles claires.
lines.en: Let's say so... provided the State finally imposes clear rules.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Là, nous sommes d'accord : sans contrainte collective, la bonne volonté ne suffit pas.
lines.en: There we agree: without collective constraint, goodwill is not enough.

days.title: La transition énergétique
days.objective: Compare energy sources and defend a position on how to power a country, weighing cost, risk and climate.
days.paretoFocus: Energy is where climate meets economics; arguing about nuclear, renewables and dependence lets you handle numbers and trade-offs at B2.
drills: State a thesis on nuclear power, give one reason for and one reason against.
Use du fait de and de sorte que in sentences about energy.
Reuse three words (le réseau, l'approvisionnement, renouvelable) in your own sentences.

items: Which soutenu phrase means because of and takes a noun?
Name one renewable and one non-renewable energy source in French.
How do you introduce a consequence in a formal register?

activities.title: Choisir son énergie
activities.intro: These sentences weigh energy options and defend a choice while granting the opposing risk. Note the nominalisations (l'approvisionnement, la dépendance) typical of formal argument.

items.hr: Miser sur le renouvelable s'impose à long terme, même si l'intermittence du vent et du soleil complique l'équilibre du réseau.
items.en: Betting on renewables is the obvious long-term choice, even if the intermittency of wind and sun complicates balancing the grid.
items.note: le réseau = the grid; l'intermittence = intermittency

items.hr: Du fait de sa dépendance aux importations, le pays reste vulnérable, de sorte qu'une production locale devient une question de souveraineté.
items.en: Because of its dependence on imports, the country stays vulnerable, so that local production becomes a matter of sovereignty.
items.note: du fait de = owing to; de sorte que = so that

items.hr: On reproche au nucléaire ses déchets, ce qui est légitime, mais il faut reconnaître qu'il émet très peu de gaz à effet de serre.
items.en: Nuclear power is blamed for its waste, which is legitimate, but one must admit it emits very little greenhouse gas.

items.hr: Rénover les logements mal isolés réduirait la consommation bien plus vite que n'importe quelle nouvelle centrale.
items.en: Renovating poorly insulated housing would cut consumption far faster than any new power plant.
items.note: isolé = insulated; une centrale = a power plant

items.hr: Quand bien même les énergies propres coûtent cher aujourd'hui, leur prix ne cesse de baisser à mesure que la demande augmente.
items.en: Even though clean energy is expensive today, its price keeps falling as demand rises.

activities.title: Peser les sources d'énergie
activities.intro: Practise energy vocabulary and the connectors of cause and consequence.

questions.prompt: Le vent et le soleil sont des énergies ___, contrairement au charbon qui finira par s'épuiser.
questions.explanation: Wind and sun are renewable; the other adjectives describe finite or dirty energy.

questions.prompt: Rappel de la cause soutenue : ___ sa dépendance au gaz étranger, le pays cherche à produire chez lui.
questions.explanation: En raison de introduces a cause; malgré would signal opposition and grâce à a favourable cause.

questions.prompt: Pour éviter les coupures, il faut équilibrer le ___ électrique entre production et consommation.
questions.explanation: Le réseau électrique is the grid that must be balanced.

questions.prompt: Rappel du conditionnel : à ma place, tu ___ (investir) plutôt dans l'isolation des bâtiments.
questions.explanation: Advice in the present uses the conditionnel présent: tu investirais.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Renewable energy is becoming increasingly competitive.'
questions.explanation: De plus en plus expresses a rising trend, framing deviennent... compétitives.

activities.title: Nucléaire ou renouvelable ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner distrusts nuclear power; grant the risk, then argue the trade-off.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Moi, le nucléaire me fait peur : un seul accident et c'est une région entière qui devient inhabitable.
lines.en: Nuclear power scares me: one accident and a whole region becomes uninhabitable.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ce risque est réel, je te l'accorde, néanmoins il reste statistiquement très rare.
lines.en: That risk is real, I grant you, nevertheless it stays statistically very rare.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et les déchets ? On les lègue aux générations futures pour des milliers d'années.
lines.en: And the waste? We leave it to future generations for thousands of years.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est le vrai problème, or le charbon, lui, tue chaque année par la pollution de l'air.
lines.en: That is the real problem, yet coal, for its part, kills every year through air pollution.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors misons tout sur le solaire et l'éolien, non ?
lines.en: Then let's bet everything on solar and wind, right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À condition d'avoir de quoi stocker l'énergie, sinon le réseau s'effondre les jours sans vent.
lines.en: Provided we can store the energy, otherwise the grid collapses on windless days.

days.title: La biodiversité et sa protection
days.objective: Argue why protecting living species matters even when it constrains human activity, granting the economic cost.
days.paretoFocus: Biodiversity loss is a core environmental theme; defending it forces you to weigh nature against jobs and to reason about long-term value.
drills: Write a thesis defending a protected area, then the objection a local worker might raise.
Use faute de and au point de in sentences about nature.
Reuse three words (une espèce, menacé, un écosystème) in your own sentences.

items: Which soutenu phrase means for lack of and takes a noun?
How do you say an endangered species in French?
Give the connector meaning to the point of.

activities.title: Préserver le vivant
activities.intro: These sentences defend biodiversity while conceding the human cost. Note the impersonal passive (il est admis que) and the abstract nouns that structure a formal argument.

items.hr: Protéger une espèce menacée n'est pas un luxe, car la disparition d'un maillon fragilise tout l'écosystème dont nous dépendons.
items.en: Protecting an endangered species is not a luxury, for the loss of one link weakens the whole ecosystem we depend on.
items.note: un maillon = a link (in a chain)

items.hr: Faute d'une réglementation stricte, les zones humides reculent au point de menacer la ressource en eau elle-même.
items.en: For lack of strict regulation, wetlands shrink to the point of threatening the water supply itself.
items.note: faute de = for lack of; au point de = to the point of

items.hr: Il est admis que l'agriculture intensive épuise les sols, quoique interdire les pesticides du jour au lendemain soit irréaliste.
items.en: It is accepted that intensive farming exhausts the soil, although banning pesticides overnight would be unrealistic.

items.hr: On invoque souvent l'emploi pour justifier la déforestation, alors qu'une forêt vivante rapporte davantage sur le long terme.
items.en: Employment is often invoked to justify deforestation, whereas a living forest yields more over the long term.
items.note: invoquer = to invoke, cite as a reason

items.hr: Rendre un territoire à la nature suppose des compromis, mais renoncer à toute protection serait bien plus coûteux à l'avenir.
items.en: Returning land to nature requires compromises, but giving up all protection would be far costlier in future.

activities.title: Défendre la nature avec nuance
activities.intro: Practise biodiversity vocabulary and the connectors of lack, consequence and concession.

questions.prompt: Le panda et bien d'autres animaux sont des espèces ___ dont la population diminue chaque année.
questions.explanation: Une espèce menacée is threatened; the sentence says the population is falling.

questions.prompt: Rappel de la concession : ___ la loi protège cette forêt, des coupes illégales continuent.
questions.explanation: Bien que introduces a concession with the subjunctive; puisque and dès que do not fit the contrast.

questions.prompt: La destruction d'un seul habitat peut déséquilibrer tout l' ___ où vivent des centaines d'espèces.
questions.explanation: Un écosystème is the whole web of living things in a place.

questions.prompt: Rappel de la cause soutenue : ___ de moyens, l'association n'a pas pu racheter la zone humide.
questions.explanation: Faute de + noun means for lack of: Faute de moyens.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The disappearance of one species threatens the whole ecosystem.'
questions.explanation: The subject La disparition d'une espèce governs the verb menace.

activities.title: Une réserve naturelle contestée
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner fears a nature reserve will cost local jobs; concede, then argue its value.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Créer une réserve ici, c'est condamner les emplois de toute la vallée.
lines.en: Creating a reserve here means dooming the jobs of the whole valley.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne nie pas le coût social, mais un territoire épuisé ne fera vivre personne longtemps.
lines.en: I don't deny the social cost, but an exhausted land will support no one for long.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Facile à dire quand ce n'est pas ton usine qui ferme.
lines.en: Easy to say when it isn't your factory closing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu as raison d'insister là-dessus, or le tourisme de nature crée aussi des emplois durables.
lines.en: You're right to press that point, yet nature tourism also creates lasting jobs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Encore faudrait-il que ces emplois arrivent avant que les anciens ne disparaissent.
lines.en: That is, provided those jobs arrive before the old ones vanish.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'où l'intérêt d'accompagner la transition, plutôt que de tout figer d'un côté ou de l'autre.
lines.en: Hence the point of supporting the transition, rather than freezing everything on one side or the other.

days.title: La surconsommation et le gaspillage
days.objective: Argue about consumer society, defending sobriety while conceding the appeal and economics of consumption.
